title: Q3 coverage checkpoint: gap remediation & commit refresh
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>, Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.ai>
Cc: Rishi Malhotra <rishi.malhotra@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Q3 coverage checkpoint: gap remediation & commit refresh

Team —

Quick reminder: finance is running the Q3 lock next Friday (11/06). We still have a ~28% gap to plan on ARR-weighted coverage for the quarter and need clean commits for the forecast. Please do the following before EOD Thursday (11/05):

- Reconcile your open deals in the attached spreadsheet (column: rep_commit) with HubSpot.
- Move any late-stage deals that do not have exec alignment or signed next steps to "best case" and reduce committed amounts accordingly.
- Confirm any large SKU/discount exceptions with me and Rishi.

Thanks — this will be tight but we can get it.

Ava Chen
Senior Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

title: Clarify VAT treatment for platform credit lines (reverse-charge and partial withholding)
preview:
From: Sophie Langston <sophie.langston@paynovo.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: accounts-payable@paynovo.com
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 09:12:00 +0000
Subject: Platform credit on invoice 20261030 — VAT treatment question

Hi Alex,

Thanks for the updated October invoice. Our finance team is holding payment for the credit lines on your invoice dated 2026-10-30 because they need confirmation of two items before we can release funds: 1) whether the platform credit lines are subject to VAT (we typically treat discounts differently), and 2) whether reverse-charge applies for Redwood in our jurisdiction.

Attached is our initial VAT registration extract for PayNovo Ltd (paynovo_vat_certificate.pdf) and a CSV showing how our AP system expects the credit lines mapped (invoice_20261030-platform-credit-lines.csv). Can you confirm the tax treatment for the three credit lines labeled PLATFORM_CREDIT_* and let us know if Redwood will issue a corrected invoice with explicit tax lines, or if we should apply reverse-charge locally?

We need this clarified by COB Wednesday (2026-11-04) so we can include payment in our weekly run. If Redwood requires any additional proof of tax residency or vendor classification, tell us what to upload.

Best,
Sophie Langston
Finance Manager — PayNovo Ltd
Sophie.Langston@paynovo.com

(attachment: paynovo_vat_certificate.pdf - PDF, 232KB)
(attachment: invoice_20261030-platform-credit-lines.csv - CSV, 6KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Langston <sophie.langston@paynovo.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 02 Nov 2026 13:04:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Platform credit on invoice 20261030 — VAT treatment question

Hi Sophie — thanks for the clear attachments, and for the deadline. Looping in Kimberly from Tax Operations and Ben from Billing so we can get a definitive answer and, if needed, a reissued invoice.

Short answer up-front: credits reduce the taxable base for Redwood's supply of services; whether VAT is shown or reverse-charge applies depends on (a) where PayNovo is VAT-registered and (b) which country is the place of supply for the billed services. From our quick scan of the certificate you sent it looks like PayNovo is VAT-registered in the UK (starts with GB). We need to confirm a couple of things with Tax Ops:
- Which tax code we applied to PLATFORM_CREDIT lines in the original invoice (taxable vs. exempt)
- Whether our system treated the credit as a separate supply (line-level tax) or as an invoice-level adjustment

Kimberly / Ben — can you confirm the tax code and whether we can reissue with line-level net/VAT columns (and indicate reverse-charge if applicable)? If reverse-charge is necessary on Redwood-originated services, we can: (1) reissue the credit portion as net 0 VAT with a note \"reverse charge — customer to account for VAT\", or (2) reissue with explicit VAT if Redwood is obliged to charge. Sophie, if you can also confirm the PayNovo VAT number exactly as shown on the certificate, that will help (we sometimes see formatting differences e.g., spaces or GB prefix).

Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive — Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai
+1 (415) 555-0147


From: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>, Sophie Langston <sophie.langston@paynovo.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 03 Nov 2026 10:22:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Platform credit on invoice 20261030 — VAT treatment question

On Mon, 02 Nov 2026 at 13:04, Alex Martinez wrote:
> Kimberly / Ben — can you confirm the tax code and whether we can reissue with line-level net/VAT columns (and indicate reverse-charge if applicable)?

Hi all — I pulled the invoice audit from our billing system. Notes below, and then proposed next steps.

Findings:
- Invoice 20261030 shows three credit lines: PLATFORM_CREDIT_TRIAL, PLATFORM_CREDIT_USAGE_ADJ, PLATFORM_CREDIT_PROMO.
- Each credit was entered as an invoice-level adjustment in our ledger (INVOICE_ADJ), not as product-level negative lines. That means the original invoice did not produce distinct VAT line items for each credit.
- Our default billing tax code for invoice-level adjustments in EU/UK region is TAXABLE_SERVICE (we applied 20% when invoice originated as UK supply). However, because this was an adjustment (negative total) the invoice rendered VAT amount as a negative VAT line aggregated into totals, which has caused some AP systems to flag ambiguity.

Recommendation / next steps:
1) Reissue a corrected invoice that moves each PLATFORM_CREDIT_* into discrete negative line items with explicit tax code and a note on each line. This makes it straightforward for AP systems to map credits to their tax treatment.
2) For PayNovo specifically: if PayNovo confirms UK VAT registration and the place-of-supply is the UK, Redwood will mark the negative line items as TAXABLE_SERVICE with VAT shown (negative). If PayNovo is VAT-registered in a different EU country and the place-of-supply is the customer location, we can instead mark the lines with the comment \"reverse charge — customer to account for VAT\" and leave VAT column blank (net amounts only).
3) We can deliver a reissued invoice as PDF + CSV by EOD tomorrow (2026-11-04) once Sophie confirms the exact VAT number format to use.

Ben — can you spin the reissue with line-level negative items and attach a CSV for PayNovo's AP import?

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Tax & Revenue Operations — Redwood Inference
kimberly.park@redwood.ai

(Quoted message preserved above)

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Sophie Langston <sophie.langston@paynovo.com>, Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.ai>
Date: Wed, 04 Nov 2026 15:20:00 +0000
Subject: Re: Platform credit on invoice 20261030 — VAT treatment question

Sophie / Alex — attached is the corrected invoice and the CSV export formatted for PayNovo's AP import. Action items completed:
- Reissued invoice 20261030-R1 (PDF) with three discrete negative line items (PLATFORM_CREDIT_TRIAL, PLATFORM_CREDIT_USAGE_ADJ, PLATFORM_CREDIT_PROMO) and line-level tax notes.
- For now, we left VAT cells empty and included the line note: \"reverse charge — customer to account for VAT\" because PayNovo confirmed a non-Redwood EU VAT registration on the certificate you supplied (GB999999999). If you prefer us to show VAT explicitly instead, tell us and we will reissue again.
- CSV file uses your mapping (columns renamed per the CSV you sent) and includes a reference column: Redwood_credit_reference=PNV-3456.

Attachments: invoice_20261030-R1.pdf (PDF, 48KB), invoice_20261030-platform-credit-lines.csv (CSV, 7KB)

Sophie — please confirm once your AP system has accepted the CSV and whether PayNovo will apply reverse-charge locally. If everything looks good we will mark the invoice as ready and you can proceed with payment.

Best,
Ben Carter
Billing Operations — Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai
(attachment: invoice_20261030-R1.pdf - PDF, 48KB)
(attachment: invoice_20261030-platform-credit-lines.csv - CSV, 7KB)

title: Invite velocity: micro-burst priority seeding & RSVP cadence
preview:
From: Lauren King <lauren.king@acme.ai>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Quick question about invite sequencing for our Oct webinar

Hi Aditya,

Thanks again for taking the time yesterday — Acme AI is excited to co-host the 'Efficient LLM Inference at Scale' session on Nov 10. Before we lock assets, a quick operational question: our demand gen lead asked whether we can do a "micro-burst" priority send to a VIP seed list (20-30 accounts) 48 hours before the main invite, and then stagger the rest of the list over three waves. They want RSVP velocity tracked closely (first 72 hours).

Can Redwood support:
- a prioritized 48-hour VIP seed send, separate tracked link and UTM
- three staggered waves for the remaining list with separate tracking
- an RSVP reminder cadence (e.g., 3 days, 24 hours, 2 hours before)

Also: any recommended subject line variants that boost opens for technical buyers?

Appreciate a quick sketch of the plan and any HubSpot/staging links to test.

Best,
Lauren
Lauren King
Senior Events Manager, Acme AI
lauren.king@acme.ai



From: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>
To: Lauren King <lauren.king@acme.ai>, Molly Torres <molly.torres@acme.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T11:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Quick question about invite sequencing for our Oct webinar

Hi Lauren — great to hear. Yes, we can absolutely do that. High-level plan below; attaching a first-pass invite (invite_v1.pdf) and a tracking map CSV with UTM variants.

Plan (micro-burst + staggered waves):
1) VIP seed (20–30 accounts) — send T-48h
   - Separate HubSpot list and a dedicated long UTM parameter: utm_campaign=invite_velocity&utm_segment=vip_seed
   - Track RSVP velocity in first 72h by account via HubSpot and our events dashboard
2) Wave A (top 1/3 remaining list) — send T-24h after VIP
3) Wave B (next 1/3) — send T+24h after Wave A
4) Wave C (final 1/3) — send T+48h after Wave B

RSVP reminder cadence (suggested):
- Reminder 1: 3 days before event (friendly reminder + highlight sessions)
- Reminder 2: 24 hours before event (logistics + calendar add)
- Reminder 3: 2 hours before event (one-tap join)

Tracking & instrumentation:
- We will create three distinct HubSpot lists and three tracking links (VIP/A/B/C) plus UTM variants for subject-line experiments.
- Add a small javascript/redirect on landing page to fire event: 'webinar_reg_submit' and pass account property if present.

Suggested subject-line variants for technical buyers (A/B test these across waves):
- "How top product teams cut per-token costs for real-time inference"
- "Scaling LLM inference: practical patterns for prod"
- "Low-latency LLM serving at scale — playbook & demo"

Next steps for us:
- Ben will assemble the subject/CTA matrix and the HubSpot link stubs.
- Laura will publish the tracking_map.csv in Drive and share staging links.
- I will run a staging send to internal test list (redwood-internal) and confirm pixel/link behavior.

Attached: invite_v1.pdf (copy for review), tracking_map.csv (UTM seeds).

On a schedule note: Ben/Laura — can you have subject matrix & hubspot links by EOD 10/22? Lauren, once you approve we can run a staging send on 10/27 and the VIP send on 11/08 (T-48h).

Thanks,
Aditya Rao
Head of Field Marketing
Redwood Inference
aditya.rao@redwood.com


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya.rao@redwood.com>, Lauren King <lauren.king@acme.ai>

Hi Lauren — great to hear. Yes, we can absolutely do that. High-level plan below; attaching a first-pass invite (invite_v1.pdf) and a tracking map CSV with UTM variants.

Plan (micro-burst + staggered waves):
1) VIP seed (20–30 accounts) — send T-48h
   - Separate HubSpot list and a dedicated long UTM parameter: utm_campaign=invite_velocity&utm_segment=vip_seed
   - Track RSVP velocity in first 72h by account via HubSpot and our events dashboard
2) Wave A (top 1/3 remaining list) — send T-24h after VIP
3) Wave B (next 1/3) — send T+24h after Wave A
4) Wave C (final 1/3) — send T+48h after Wave B

RSVP reminder cadence (suggested):
- Reminder 1: 3 days before event (friendly reminder + highlight sessions)
- Reminder 2: 24 hours before event (logistics + calendar add)
- Reminder 3: 2 hours before event (one-tap join)

Tracking & instrumentation:
- We will create three distinct HubSpot lists and three tracking links (VIP/A/B/C) plus UTM variants for subject-line experiments.
- Add a small javascript/redirect on landing page to fire event: 'webinar_reg_submit' and pass account property if present.

Suggested subject-line variants for technical buyers (A/B test these across waves):
- "How top product teams cut per-token costs for real-time inference"
- "Scaling LLM inference: practical patterns for prod"
- "Low-latency LLM serving at scale — playbook & demo"

- Ben will assemble the subject/CTA matrix and the HubSpot link stubs.
- Laura will publish the tracking_map.csv in Drive and share staging links.
- I will run a staging send to internal test list (redwood-internal) and confirm pixel/link behavior.

Hi Irene,

Thanks for the quick intro via the partner portal. We're ready to start the marketplace onboarding for AzureSphere's managed inference offering. To unblock listing and PO, I've attached our signed W-9 and the bank wiring instructions.

Attachments:
- AzureSphere_W9.pdf (application/pdf)
- AZS_wiring_instructions.pdf (application/pdf)

High-level ask: can Redwood accept a single monthly PO via the Azure Marketplace (transactable listing) or do we need a private offer/PO direct to Azure? Also, which procurement forms do you need from us for marketplace activation and for your finance team to route a PO?

Happy to hop on a short call this week — our procurement lead is cc'd.

Best,
Lisa Grant
Head of Finance
AzureSphere, Inc.
procurement@azuresphere.ai
+1 (415) 555-0132

From: Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>
To: Lisa Grant <lisa.grant@azuresphere.ai>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa.ortiz@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly.park@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-13T10:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace onboarding: AzureSphere - procurement intake

Hi Lisa — thanks for sending the W-9 and wiring stub. Quick answers up front: Redwood can support both a transactable Azure Marketplace listing and a private offer flow, but the PO routing differs and we'll need a couple of items from you to determine the cleanest path.

What we need from AzureSphere to move to intake and PO routing:
- Completed vendor intake form (doc attached) with exact legal entity name, EIN, registered address, and remittance details.
- SOC 2 Type II attestation (summary or link to report) or equivalent security evidence.
- Primary billing contact and email for invoicing and PO validation.
- Confirmation of desired marketplace path: transactable listing on Azure Marketplace or private offer via Azure CSP/partner channel.

If you choose a transactable Azure listing, Azure billing maps to buyer accounts via marketplace invoicing and Redwood will need the payor mapping details to set up billing. For a private offer, we typically accept a PO issued to Redwood with vendor payor mapping in our finance portal; that process requires a bit more paperwork but is straightforward.

I attached our vendor_intake_form.docx. Please return a completed copy plus any SOC 2/Security summary and your preferred go-live target.

If helpful, I can schedule a 30-minute intake call with Vanessa (marketplace ops) and Ben (finops) to walk through the PO routing.

Thanks,
Irene
Head, Marketplace Partnerships
Redwood Inference

title: Coordination: model deprecation notice & customer exception flow (publication latch)
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Coordination: model deprecation notice & customer exception flow (publication latch)

Team —

TL;DR: We need to lock a comms publication plan for the October->November model family deprecation, define the customer exception (exemption) intake & review process, and confirm who owns the ‘publication latch’ (i.e., the gating decision that prevents notices from going live until all engineering & security checks are green).

Asks:
- Marissa: draft timeline for customer notice, emails, and portal banner. Target publish week: 11/02. Can you confirm slack cadence for announcement to sales/CS?
- Irene: tech constraints — what hard blockers exist for listing recommended migration variants, sequencing for traffic-shift opt-ins, and visible SDK deprecation headers?
- Ben: ops readiness — who owns rollback hatch, pager coverage for the week of publication, and a quick playbook if we get >3 critical tickets in 24h?
- Naomi: comms copy — I attached a short outline. Please produce a 1-page customer-facing FAQ + snippet for Sales one-pager by Thursday.

We also need to finalize the exception flow: intake form, required evidence, SLA for review (48h standard), and escalation to the Exceptions Board for non-standard asks. I propose a simple two-tier approach: auto-approve short-term (<=30 days) infra-only exemptions, board review for anything longer or feature-impacting.

I'll coordinate a 30-min sync tomorrow to lock owners unless you have objections.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Product Manager, Platform
Redwood Inference

Attachment: customer_notice_draft_v1.pdf (summary draft)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.com>, Irene Choi <irene.choi@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Naomi Feldman <naomi.feldman@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 20 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Coordination: model deprecation notice & customer exception flow (publication latch)

Thanks Alex — short answers below and a proposed calendar.

- Publication week: I can push the customer email + portal banner on 2026-11-02. Pre-announcement (to Sales & CS) the week of 10/26 with a 15-min town hall on 10/28.
- Slack cadence: Post in #prod-announce and pin in #sales-ops. I will prepare a 1-slide TL;DR for product drop-in.
- Comms artifacts: Naomi and I will co-own the customer FAQ. Naomi to finalize copy by Thu (10/22).
- Publication latch: propose that the latch is owned by Product (Alex) in coordination with SRE (Ben) and Security (Irene). The latch checklist: successful canary + no critical regressions in perf tests + security sign-off.

I attached a proposed comms calendar and a draft FAQ outline.

— Marissa

title: Intermediary seller chain-of-custody (CoC) & resale certificate — invoice hold
preview:
From: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:20:00-07:00
Subject: Invoice 847 hold — missing intermediary chain-of-custody

Hi Aisha,

Quick note from Marketplace billing — we received Redwood invoice #847 for Marketplace Inc (remit to: Gina Lopez / Wholesale Co.) but the buyer (intermediary seller ID 9987) provided a resale certificate covering Sales Tax only at the end-customer level. Our compliance team is requesting chain-of-custody documentation that ties the reseller (the marketplace seller) to the shipped goods / billed entity.

Specifically they're asking for one of the following:
- a signed POA/letter from the reseller authorizing Marketplace to act as the seller of record and confirming resale coverage,
- a ledger export showing the intermediary seller->end-customer flow for this SKU (invoice range / dates), or
- a notarized resale certificate explicitly listing the intermediary seller entity and the Marketplace as facilitating entity.

Until we receive acceptable CoC evidence, Marketplace has placed the invoice on hold for tax clearance and final payment. Can you confirm whether Redwood can supply any of the above or if Marketplace will accept a digitally signed affidavit from the seller?

Thanks,
Marketplace Ops
[Marketplace Inc — Billing]

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
To: Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Tessa Morgan <tessa_morgan@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-19T10:12:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Invoice 847 hold — missing intermediary chain-of-custody

Thanks for the heads-up — looping in revenue ops and the account owner to triage.

Ben / Tessa — Marketplace is asking for chain-of-custody documentation for invoice 847 tied to intermediary seller ID 9987. Invoice details: PO# MP-847, Gross USD 42,300, shipped 2026-09-28. The seller (Gina Lopez / Wholesale Co.) submitted a state resale certificate that covers retail sales, but it does not name Marketplace or show transfer of title.

Current ask: can Redwood provide any of the three items Marketplace listed? If not, do we have authority to request a signed affidavit from Wholesale Co. that covers the transaction and names Marketplace as the facilitating seller? This is blocking the payment — accounting flagged it as a payment collection risk.

If we can get a ledger export (seller-side) mapping the sale to the end-customer, that would be fastest. If the seller will only provide a certificate that names their own EIN, Marketplace may still reject it.

Next steps I propose:
- Ben to contact Wholesale Co. procurement (Gina) to request a signed POA or ledger export.
- Tessa to prepare a standard Redwood affidavit template Redwood can share if seller prefers digital attestation.
- Karthik to advise on tax acceptance policy (multi-state) and whether a redline is acceptable.

I'll follow up tomorrow with an update.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive — Strategic Sales
Redwood Inference
[aisha_rahman@redwood.com | +1-415-555-0132]

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>
To: Gina Lopez <gina.lopez@wholesaler.com>
Cc: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>, Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>, Karthik Iyer <karthik_iyer@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-20T08:45:00-07:00
Subject: Request: POA or ledger export to clear invoice 847 (urgent)

Hi Gina — hope you're well. Ben here from Redwood (cc'd Aisha). Marketplace has placed invoice #847 on hold due to missing chain-of-custody (CoC) documentation for the sale that flowed through Marketplace seller ID 9987.

To help Marketplace release payment, could Wholesale Co. provide one of the following as soon as possible:
1) Signed POA / assignment letter from Wholesale Co. authorizing Marketplace Inc to sell on your behalf for the transaction(s) covered by PO# MP-847; OR
2) A CSV ledger export showing the transaction chain (seller invoice -> marketplace facilitation -> end-customer invoice) for the SKU(s) on MP-847; OR
3) A notarized or digitally signed resale certificate that explicitly lists Wholesale Co. (EIN) and indicates the Marketplace as the intermediary facilitating the sale.

For convenience I attached a quick ledger stub from Marketplace with the seller ID and dates — if you can sign/return a POA that references the PO# and ship dates, that should suffice.

Attachment: marketplace-seller-ledger-20261019.csv (CSV, 32KB)

If you prefer Redwood to share a short affidavit template for the seller to sign, we can send it; our Revenue Ops team (Karthik) is copied for tax acceptability review.

Appreciate a quick turnaround — Marketplace indicated the funds will be released once documentation is received.

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Revenue Operations — Redwood Inference
[ben_carter@redwood.com | +1-650-555-0199]

From: Gina Lopez <gina.lopez@wholesaler.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.com>, Marketplace Ops <ops@marketplace-inc.com>
Cc: thirdparty-contracts@3rdparty.com, Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.com>
Date: 2026-10-21T15:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Request: POA or ledger export to clear invoice 847 (urgent)

Hi Ben / team,

Thanks for the clear instructions — please find attached the seller's resale certificate and a ledger export showing the seller-side transaction for PO# MP-847. The resale cert is a state-level form (TX) and includes Wholesale Co.'s EIN; it does not name Marketplace explicitly.

Attachments:
- gina_resale_cert_2026-10-20.pdf [PDF, 210KB] (signed by Gina Lopez)
- marketplace-seller-ledger-20261019.csv [CSV, 32KB] (shows seller invoice #S-2201 mapping to MP-847)

We are willing to sign the Redwood affidavit if Marketplace requires the reseller to name Marketplace in a POA. Please share the template and we will get it signed today.

Gina Lopez
Wholesale Co. — Head of Sales Operations
[gina.lopez@wholesaler.com | +1-312-555-0202]

On acceptance: if Redwood/Marketplace confirm the ledger + the signed resale cert are sufficient, we will consider the POA as a backup to speed payment.

preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava@redwoodinference.com>
To: Jordan Ellis <jordan@clearwave.com>, Priya Sundar <psundar@clearwave.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-10-18T09:02:00-07:00
Subject: Executive attendance bandwidth + compact demo hold for ClearWave

Hi Jordan & Priya,

Thanks again for the time yesterday — excited to move to a technical demo to show throughput and the routing controls we discussed. Two asks up front: can your VP of Engineering or CIO join the first 30–45 minutes? We find having a senior sponsor in the opening block helps map requirements to the right SLA/isolated deployment options.

Proposed approach:
- 45-minute compact demo (30 live + 15 Q&A) with a 5-minute stakeholder sync at the start for goals and constraints.
- We'll tailor the first 10 minutes to ops/security items (data residency, audit logs).
- Ben will run the runtime deep-dive; Marissa will cover rollout/SLAs.

Windows we can hold right now (all PT):
- Tue 10/20: 10:00–10:45
- Wed 10/21: 09:00–09:45 or 11:30–12:15

Attached: Demo_Agenda_v3.pdf, Mutual_Action_Plan_ClearWave_v1.xlsx. I'm dropping a 45min hold (45min-demo.ics) tentatively for Wed 11:30 PT so we don't lose the slot — happy to move any of the proposed windows.

If a VP/CIO cannot make the live opening, would the CTO join for the first 20 minutes and we invite the CIO to the follow-up? Open to suggestions.

Best,
Ava Chen
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachments: Demo_Agenda_v3.pdf (application/pdf), Mutual_Action_Plan_ClearWave_v1.xlsx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet), 45min-demo.ics (text/calendar)

From: Jordan Ellis <jordan@clearwave.com>

title: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 14 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Liam / CardioHealth team — attaching an activation dependency matrix for the POC onramp. This is a lightweight MAP-focused doc that lays out the minimum sequencing we need to hit a production-like test in your staging (API keys, org + tenant setup, IP allowlist, and VPC peering).

Top-level asks: 
- Confirm the IP CIDR ranges we should add to the allowlist (we've proposed 52.22.44.0/24 and 18.197.128.0/25 as a short-lived window for the first two sprints).
- Will CardioHealth initiate the VPC peering request or should Redwood create a peering and share the ID (we can support both; if you prefer to initiate, share the contact).
- Org setup: we need a tenant name and an owner email (read/write for first 30d).
- Ephemeral API key pattern: propose 72-hour TTL during eval; confirm acceptable.

Attached: activation-dependency-matrix.xlsx (sheet 1 = milestones, sheet 2 = owners/contact points).

Proposed milestone cadence (high level):
1) Org + owner assigned (day 0)
2) Short-lived API key + smoke test (day 1)
3) IP allowlist applied + test (day 1–2)
4) VPC peering (optional, day 3–6)
5) Canary load + evaluation queries (day 7–14)

If you want, Jonas can walk through the matrix with your netops team tomorrow — propose a 30-min slot.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Solutions Engineer — Redwood Inference
---
Attachment: activation-dependency-matrix.xlsx (spreadsheet stub, 56KB)


From: Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: netops@cardiohealth.com
Date: Thu, 14 Oct 2026 11:01:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

Ava — thanks for the matrix and clear asks. A few quick notes from NetOps: 
- CIDR ranges: we can open the 52.22.44.0/24 and 18.197.128.0/25 windows for an initial 72-hour trial, but will need the exact source IPs once sessions stabilize.
- VPC peering: CardioHealth typically accepts incoming peering requests (we prefer to initiate to keep our routing strict). I'll have netops create the request if you provide the Redwood VPC ID and proposed routing rules.
- Org owner: use onboarding@cardiohealth.com as the temporary owner and we will switch to the permanent owner when legal finalizes the tenant agreement.
- Ephemeral keys: 72h TTL is fine for the trial. We will need instructions on rotating keys if you expect rolling restarts.

MM: netops@cardiohealth.com — I'll leave you to coordinate peering logistics with Jonas.

I'll confirm the peering window after sync with netops; tentatively aiming for Mon Oct 18 to Thu Oct 21.

— Liam
Head of Integrations, CardioHealth Labs

Jonas — want to own the peering kickoff and the rotation doc? I'll set a standing 15-min checkpoint during the activation window.

Ben
Account Executive — Redwood Inference


From: Jonas Weber <jonas.weber@redwood.com>
To: netops@cardiohealth.com, Liam O'Neil <liam.oneil@cardiohealth.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Evelyn Hart <evelyn.hart@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 18 Oct 2026 09:34:00 -0700
Subject: Re: POC activation dependency matrix & onramp run-through

NetOps team / Liam — thanks. Quick technical plan and the minimal artifacts we'll need from CardioHealth to create the peering:
- Required from CardioHealth (NetOps): your VPC ID, the CIDR you will advertise, and a primary contact for route propagation acceptance.
- Required from Redwood: Redwood VPC ID (redwood-prod-eval-1), suggested routing (no default-route export), and a time window for BGP handshake.

Operational notes: 
- We'll provision an ephemeral API key scoped to the eval tenant with 72h TTL. It will be delivered through a signed Doc link (or HubSpot secure file) and rotated automatically after expiry.
- For the allowlist smoke test: provide a simple curl command we can run from our side to hit your staging endpoint once IPs are added. Sample: curl -H 'Authorization: Bearer <api-key>' https://staging.cardiohealth.com/v1/ping
- If CardioHealth prefers to initiate peering, we can share the Terraform snippet and the Redwood peer ASN. Otherwise, we will open the peering request and share the request id for tracking.

Proposed immediate next steps: 
1) Liam / NetOps confirm peering initiation preference and provide VPC ID (today).
2) CardioHealth open the suggested allowlist windows (if you want us to run smoke tests in the window, tell us the ETA).
3) Redwood publishes ephemeral key and sample curl for smoke (once org owner onboarding@cardiohealth.com is confirmed).

Can we do a 30-min call today at 3:00 PM PT to run through how we rotate keys and how we will validate routing? I will share a short runbook during the call.

Jonas Weber
Network & Integrations — Redwood Inference
---

title: Seat flexibility + SDR training retainer approval for engagement tooling
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Cc: Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Mon, 12 Oct 2026 09:14:00 -0700
Subject: Seat flexibility + SDR training retainer approval for engagement tooling

Hi team,

We have the Outreach renewal approaching (term ends 11/30) and I wanted to propose a slightly different approach than a straight seat license renewal. Summary of proposal: 

- Purchase a base committed seat pool for 120 users and a 20-seat flex buffer (seat credits that can be reassigned monthly).
- Add a 6-month enablement retainer (SDR bootcamp + playbook implementation, up to 10 days of vendor trainer time).
- Net effect should reduce churn overhead and speed ramp for new hires; conservative cost estimate attached.

Attachments: Outreach_Quote_20261012.xlsx, SOW_SDREnablement_v1.pdf, CostComparison_EngagementTools.csv

Question for procurement/finance: can we route part of the retainer to FY27 professional services (to align with hiring plan) and treat seat flex as subscription? Ben, I can draft allocation if you confirm the fund code.

Why this hybrid: our SDR headcount is projected to fluctuate with Q1 hiring; having a small flex pool avoids multiple seat reassignments and refresh tickets. The retainer keeps the vendors responsible for a faster completion of training and DB of playbooks.

Next steps I propose:
1) Procurement to sign off on allocation and PO path (internal)
2) Ask Jordan (Outreach) for a DocuSign-ready quote with net-30 invoice terms and explicit seat-flex mechanics
3) Legal to run the SOW and confirm standard data security annex

Ping me if you'd prefer Salesloft out of the gate instead — I did include a cost row in the comparison but Outreach has the better implementation bundle right now.

Thanks,
Alex Martinez
Senior Sales Ops Manager
Redwood Inference

---
[attachment: Outreach_Quote_20261012.xlsx] [attachment: SOW_SDREnablement_v1.pdf] [attachment: CostComparison_EngagementTools.csv]

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Soojin Lee <soojin.lee@redwood.ai>
Date: Tue, 13 Oct 2026 10:02:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Seat flexibility + SDR training retainer approval for engagement tooling

Alex —

Looks reasonable, a few clarifying items from procurement perspective before we greenlight: 

1) Seat-flex accounting: we need concrete language on how credits are consumed and returned month-to-month (audit trail). Can Jordan add an appendix in the quote?
2) Retainer deliverables: the SOW is high level. I need a slot-level deliverable schedule and acceptance criteria (so we can avoid open-ended hours).
3) Payment: Finance prefers net-30 with milestone invoicing for the retainer (25% on kickoff, 50% mid, 25% delivery). Ben — is that workable?
4) Security: can we get a standard vendor security questionnaire response (SOC 2 attestation + encryption notes)?

If procurement signs off, we should execute before 10/28 to allow a 30-day onboarding window.

Marissa

title: MSA / Order Form — staggered payment ladder, invoice recipient & VAT allocation
preview:
From: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Marta Ruiz <marta.ruiz@starlite-eu.com>, finance@starlite-eu.com
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Hi Alex,

Thanks for sending over the draft order form. A few quick questions from our side to make sure procurement & Treasury can wire things correctly:

1) Payment cadence: Starlite prefers staged invoices tied to delivery milestones. You proposed three tranches over 12 months — can you confirm the invoice dates align to usage start rather than signature date? We need dates for cashflow planning.

2) Invoice recipient & legal entity: We need the invoice sent to Starlite Technologies BV (EU entity) for VAT purposes. Is Redwood able to provide entity-level invoicing (US entity for the upfront, EU for recurring)?

3) VAT liability: For our EU entity we typically apply reverse charge where applicable. Will Redwood accept reverse charge on recurring tokens, or do you require us to provide VAT registration evidence first?

I attached our PO so you can see the internal reference. Once we confirm, I can route a formal PO and start the DocuSign flow.

Appreciate a quick sync — our CFO would like this decided before the month-end.

Best,
Emma Saunders
Head of Procurement
Starlite Technologies

Attachment: PO_Starlite_20261010.pdf (application/pdf, 115KB)

From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
To: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 12 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Hi Emma — thanks for the clear note and the PO. Looping in Ben (sales lead) and Kimberly (legal) so we can align on the order form redlines.

Short answers up front:
- Payment dates: we can tie invoice triggers to "start of use" milestones. For the three-tranche model I recommend: invoice 1 at signature (upfront onboarding credit), invoice 2 at 30 days post-start, invoice 3 at 180 days post-start. If you need fixed calendar dates instead we can map those in the order form.
- Entity-level invoicing: we can accept split invoicing (US entity for upfront, Redwood EU subsidiary for recurrent). Need a legal name and remit-to address for the EU entity on the order form.
- VAT / reverse charge: we routinely accept reverse charge where applicable, but we require VAT evidence (VAT number + certificate) prior to applying reverse-charge treatment on recurring invoices. Kimberly — can you confirm the clause & the evidence checklist?

I've uploaded a redline of the order form with placeholders for remit-to and the tranche triggers: Starlite_OrderForm_redline.docx (attached). Please review and flag any objections.

Next steps I propose:
1) Emma to confirm remit-to entity name and VAT number.
2) Kimberly to provide the specific evidence items we require (short checklist).
3) Ben & I to update billing triggers in the OF and start DocuSign once redlines are accepted.

Thanks — happy to jump on a 20m call if helpful.

— Alex Martinez
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

Attachment: Starlite_OrderForm_redline.docx (application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document, 48KB)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Cc: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 13 Oct 2026 09:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Emma — quick confirmation from Sales/Finance side: we can accept the proposed split invoicing and the staged cadence Alex described. Net terms for the 2nd and 3rd invoices would be Net45 from invoice date (we can discuss Net30 if Treasury prefers but that will affect pricing).

On VAT: we do require the VAT number + a signed VAT declaration for the reverse charge to be applied. If Starlite can provide that, we will mark the recurring invoice lines as reverse-charge and exclude VAT on our EU invoices.

Kimberly — can you share the minimum wording we should include in the Order Form (VAT/Tax clause) and attach the checklist for Emma?

Ben Carter
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

(1-line reply expected)

From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Emma Saunders <emma@starlite-ops.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 15 Oct 2026 14:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Order flow questions — payment ladder, invoice recipient, VAT

Thanks for the context — attaching a clean MSA redline and a VAT evidence checklist for Starlite. Key points we added: 

- Invoice triggers: we added explicit milestone language \"Start of Use\" defined as the first production inference request to Redwood's hosted API endpoint for the named tenant. That maps the invoice triggers to measurable events.
- Entity invoicing: Order Form now supports \"Per-Entity Remit\" where the Buyer specifies the remit-to legal entity and address for each invoice line. We included a field for the EU entity details you referenced.
- VAT: clause 7.3: \"Where Buyer is registered for VAT in an EU jurisdiction and provides valid VAT registration details and a signed VAT declaration, Seller will apply reverse charge treatment to recurring service fees. Seller reserves the right to re-bill VAT if evidence is invalid or jurisdiction rules require collection.\"

Attachments included: redline_msa_clean.docx, vat-evidence-checklist.pdf — the checklist lists: VAT number (copy), VAT registration certificate (translated if not English), and a signed VAT reverse-charge declaration on company letterhead.

If Emma can send the EU entity remit-to details and the VAT docs, Ben & Alex should be able to proceed with the DocuSign bundle by EOD Monday. If Treasury needs a specific invoice schedule (fixed dates instead of milestone-based), tell us the preferred dates and we'll lock them into the OF.

Kimberly Park
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference

From: Marta Jimenez <marta.jimenez@silverlinehealth.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: procurement@silverlinehealth.com
Date: Wed, 7 Oct 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: PO fields, net60 request and monthly split invoices

Hi Aditya,

Thanks for the demo last week — the team at Silverline is keen to move forward. Procurement has one set of asks before we can issue the official PO:

- We need confirmation of which PO header fields Redwood requires (we have separate ERP mappings that require exact header names).
- Silverline standard payment is net60 — can you accept net60 on the PO?
- We prefer monthly split invoices (one invoice per month per billing entity) rather than consolidated quarterly invoices.
- Also, are there packaging/commitment discounts if we commit to the platform for 12 months (approx. $250,000 ARR)? If so, please outline eligibility and any invoice cadence constraints tied to discounts.

I attached a short PDF with our PO template and the fields that our ERP will populate (Silverline_PO_Req.pdf). Please let me know what you need on your side so procurement can issue the PO.

Thanks,
Marta
Marta Jimenez
Sr. Procurement Manager | Silverline Health
procurement@silverlinehealth.com

From: Aditya Rao <aditya_rao@redwoodinference.com>
To: Marta Jimenez <marta.jimenez@silverlinehealth.com>
Cc: Vanessa Ortiz <vanessa_ortiz@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 7 Oct 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: PO fields, net60 request and monthly split invoices

Hi Marta — great to hear and thanks for sharing the PO template. Short answers up front, then a suggested path: 

1) PO header fields: we require the following exact fields for ERP matching: PO Number, Bill-To Entity (legal entity name), Bill-To Address, Tax ID / EIN, Billing Contact (email), Invoice Frequency, Currency, and Redwood Vendor Code. If Silverline wants invoice line-level SKU mapping include 'SKU' and 'Line Description'. I'll attach a Redwood invoice template (redwood-invoice-template.xlsx) so you can see the export format our AR team consumes.

2) Net terms: Our standard for new enterprise netting is net45. We can make an exception to net60, subject to a quick credit review and a D&B/References check. If procurement can accept a one-cycle net60 (first three invoices) and then net45 thereafter, that is something Finance will allow to mitigate risk. Alternatively, we can offer net60 on the condition of a 5% prepayment to offset working capital — happy to discuss.

3) Monthly split invoices: Yes, we can issue one invoice per month per billing entity. We just need the PO to indicate the invoice frequency and the PO should reference the Billing Entity code that matches your ERP. We cannot accept ad-hoc line-item remapping after invoices are issued (it creates reconciliation overhead).

4) Discount & packaging: Discount eligibility is tied to committed ARR, billing cadence, and whether invoices are issued net30/net45 vs net60. For a 12-month commitment at $250k ARR with monthly invoicing, our standard commercial can include a 5% committed-volume discount. Higher discounts (7-10%) are possible if Silverline agrees to quarterly advance billing or a partial upfront prepayment schedule.

Proposed next steps (mutual action plan):
- You confirm the exact Bill-To Entity name and PO header sample (I can validate against our template).
- We run a quick credit check and Finance gives conditional sign-off for net60 for the first three billing cycles.
- We issue a contract amendment reflecting the payment terms and the 5% volume discount; both sides sign via DocuSign.
- Procurement issues PO referencing Redwood Vendor Code: RW-2311 and the Bill-To Entity code.

I'm attaching the redwood-invoice-template.xlsx and will loop in Finance internally to expedite the netterms conversation.

Talk soon,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise Sales | Redwood Inference
aditya_rao@redwoodinference.com
(415) 555-0183

---

Cc: Grace O'Connor <grace_oconnor@redwoodinference.com>

Ben / Vanessa — forwarding Silverline's PO request. Key asks: net60, monthly split invoices, and they are looking for a 12-month pricing commitment (~$250k). I proposed a 5% committed-volume discount for monthly invoicing and mentioned the option of a higher discount for quarterly advance billing.

Subject: Re: PO fields, net60 request and monthly split invoices

Hi Marta — great to hear and thanks for sharing the PO template. Short answers up front, then a suggested path: 

1) PO header fields: we require the following exact fields for ERP matching: PO Number, Bill-To Entity (legal entity name), Bill-To Address, Tax ID / EIN, Billing Contact (email), Invoice Frequency, Currency, and Redwood Vendor Code. If Silverline wants invoice line-level SKU mapping include 'SKU' and 'Line Description'. I'll attach a Redwood invoice template (redwood-invoice-template.xlsx) so you can see the export format our AR team consumes.

2) Net terms: Our standard for new enterprise netting is net45. We can make an exception to net60, subject to a quick credit review and a D&B/References check. If procurement can accept a one-cycle net60 (first three invoices) and then net45 thereafter, that is something Finance will allow to mitigate risk. Alternatively, we can offer net60 on the condition of a 5% prepayment to offset working capital — happy to discuss.

3) Monthly split invoices: Yes, we can issue one invoice per month per billing entity. We just need the PO to indicate the invoice frequency and the PO should reference the Billing Entity code that matches your ERP. We cannot accept ad-hoc line-item remapping after invoices are issued (it creates reconciliation overhead).

4) Discount & packaging: Discount eligibility is tied to committed ARR, billing cadence, and whether invoices are issued net30/net45 vs net60. For a 12-month commitment at $250k ARR with monthly invoicing, our standard commercial can include a 5% committed-volume discount. Higher discounts (7-10%) are possible if Silverline agrees to quarterly advance billing or a partial upfront prepayment schedule.

Proposed next steps (mutual action plan):
- You confirm the exact Bill-To Entity name and PO header sample (I can validate against our template).
- We run a quick credit check and Finance gives conditional sign-off for net60 for the first three billing cycles.
- We issue a contract amendment reflecting the payment terms and the 5% volume discount; both sides sign via DocuSign.
- Procurement issues PO referencing Redwood Vendor Code: RW-2311 and the Bill-To Entity code.

I'm attaching the redwood-invoice-template.xlsx and will loop in Finance internally to expedite the netterms conversation.

Talk soon,
Aditya Rao

title: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options
preview:
From: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
To: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 05 Oct 2026 08:12:00 -0700
Subject: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Hi Jenna,

Thanks again for the time last week. I pulled together two straightforward options for Slate's upcoming renewal based on the utilization profile for Q3 (attached):

Current baseline: 40 reserved GPUs (A100 equiv), 24/7. Observed patterns: sustained 60-70% utilization on weekdays with frequent 90%+ mini-spikes during model retrain windows and nightly batch jobs. Tail latency increases when short (1–2 hour) peak waves occur.

Option A — Conservative uplift + burst: +9 reserved GPUs (49 total) + 16-GPU burst pool for peak windows. This keeps monthly committed spend close to today while covering observed peaks with burst capacity.

Option B — Phased reserve step-up: +12 reserved GPUs immediately (52 total), 3-month review to consider moving 6 of those into a scheduled burst pool if average utilization normalizes. This is the lower operational risk path for latency-sensitive workloads.

Short ask: can you confirm which appetite Slate has for committed spend vs. relying on burst credits? If you prefer, we can run a 6‑week controlled pilot for Option A (monitoring SLOs and unit economics) before committing to Option B.

Attached: slate_q3_utilization.csv, draft_amendment_slate_v2.pdf (proposal + cost delta table).

If it helps, I can set up a 30-minute sync with our solutions + finance folks to walk through the cost math and SLO guarantees.

Thanks,
Kimberly Park
Account Executive, Redwood Inference
kimberly@redwood.com
Redwood Inference

--
Attachment: slate_q3_utilization.csv (type: text/csv)
Attachment: draft_amendment_slate_v2.pdf (type: application/pdf)

From: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 06 Oct 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Kimberly,

Appreciate the breakdown and the files. A few quick notes from our side:

- Procurement timeline: our FY27 budget window opens Oct 20; we need a signed SOW by Oct 25 to hit the Q4 spend plan.
- Cost sensitivity: we can stretch committed spend a bit but prefer not to double the baseline without clear ROI.
- Risk tolerance: latency-sensitive prod routes can't rely solely on on-demand bursting; predictable headroom preferred.

Given that, I'm leaning hybrid — something close to Option A but with a smaller immediate commit uplift and a well-defined review cadence. Before we sign anything, procurement will need the delta numbers broken down monthly and a simple SLA addendum for latency behavior during peak windows.

Can Ben or someone on your side run a 90‑day projected cost / SLO impact for a +9 reserved GPU scenario with a 16-GPU burst pool? Also please include how we would handle short retrain spikes (2–3 hours) and any automatic failovers if the burst pool is saturated.

Looping in our procurement alias (procurement@slate.ai) for visibility.

Thanks,
Jenna Ortiz
Head of Platform, Slate AI
jenna@slate.ai

On Mon, Oct 5, 2026 at 08:12 AM Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com> wrote:
> [original message]


From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>, Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>
Cc: Karthik Iyer <karthik@redwood.com>
Date: Wed, 07 Oct 2026 10:45:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Hey Jenna — quick cost + ops sketch attached (also dropped a short calc in the Drive link). Key points: 

- Measured tail: 95th-percentile token latency climbs ~40% when aggregate utilization >80% because of queuing in the serving pool. That explains the customer-reported latency blips.
- For immediate protection we recommend +10 reserved GPUs (49 -> 50 assuming rounding) paired with an auto-burst window sized to 16 GPUs that kicks in when the 5-min rolling utilization exceeds 72%. That gives ~2.5x more thermal headroom for 1–3 hour spikes.
- Cost impact: our model shows ~+11% monthly committed spend vs current for +10 GPUs, with a blended spend (committed + burst) ~+18% for the blended months we assume spikes. If you want to cap budget delta, we can propose a staged billing (first 3 months at blended rate, then reprice based on realized hours).
- Operational guardrails: automatic fallback to smaller model instances when burst is saturated, plus prioritized routing for your prod routes. We also recommend a 6-week pilot on a subset of prod traffic to verify tail behavior before full cutover.

I'll attach the calculator and a one-pager SLO amendment for sign-off.

Ben Carter
Senior Solutions Engineer, Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com

--
Attachment: slate_cost_calc_q3_v1.xlsx (type: application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet)

From: Jenna Ortiz <jenna@slate.ai>
To: Kimberly Park <kimberly@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Cc: Procurement <procurement@slate.ai>
Date: Fri, 08 Oct 2026 15:40:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Slate AI renewal — commit bridging & sizing options

Kimberly/Ben,

Thanks — this is helpful. Procurement has reviewed the initial numbers and is willing to proceed on a hybrid path:

- Agreement: add +9 reserved GPUs to the existing commit (total 49).
- Burst: enable a 16‑GPU burst pool for up to 120 hours/month.
- Pilot & review: 6‑week pilot starting on activation, with a retro review to re-evaluate whether 3 of the reserved GPUs can be reclassified into burst capacity.
- SLA addendum: procure will require the latency SLO addendum for prod routes (please attach final language to the SOW).

Next steps from our side: procurement needs an updated SOW and a DocuSign link by Oct 20 to route internally. Please include the monthly delta table and a contact for technical escalation.

Procurement contact: Maya Lin <maya@slate.ai> (cc’d).

If you can get the SOW + DocuSign before EOD next Tuesday (Oct 12), we’ll prioritize the internal approval.

Best,
Jenna Ortiz
Head of Platform, Slate AI

- Approval thresholds (current): up to 25% senior AE/AM; 25-35% requires VP; 35-50% requires CFO; >50% requires CEO sign-off.

- Alex: send any customer references / strategic rationale for priority handling
- Lauren: will you please indicate whether CFO sign-off is required for this one-time construct?

title: Post-call: staggered technical deep-dive — three-shift scheduling options
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Marta Silva <marta.silva@medsys.io>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 08:15:00 -0400
Subject: Post-call: staggered technical deep-dive — three-shift scheduling options

Hi Marta — thanks again for a great discovery call this morning. Quick recap and suggested next steps so we can lock in a tech deep-dive: 

Summary from call:
- Use case: secure LLM inference for clinical-note summarization and search across patient records (PHI-in-scope).
- Primary constraints: on-prem/private deployment requirement in EMEA, strict audit logging, SLA window for evening batch processing.
- Success metric: 95th pct latency < 300ms for user-facing queries; embedding throughput for nightly jobs.

Suggested next step: 60–90 minute technical deep-dive with our infra and security engineers to walk architecture, compliance checklist, and a short sandbox demo. To make this work for your global teams we sketched a "three-shift" rotating schedule (US / EU / APAC) so stakeholders can attend at reasonable hours on a recurring cadence. Attached is a simple availability grid and one-page proposal for rotating slots.

Proposed windows (first pass):
- US-friendly slot: Tue 10:00–11:30 ET (good for east coast teams)
- EU-friendly slot: Wed 14:00–15:30 CET (aligns with EMEA technical owners)
- APAC-friendly slot: Thu 09:00–10:30 SGT (morning APAC block)

Options for cadence — please indicate preferred:
1) Weekly rotation: same week alternate region (fast turnaround for POC teams).
2) Fixed-week blocks: e.g., two weeks per region then rotate (less context switching).
3) Monthly rotating slot for exec/architect review, weekly region-specific deep-dive for engineering.

Next actions: can you confirm which cadence you prefer and any absolute blackout dates (internal compliance review next month, etc.)? I put a Calendly link in the follow-up for each slot and attached an availability grid (availability_grid_proposal.xlsx).

Thanks — happy to adjust.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Solutions Engineer | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com
+1 (415) 555-0119

---
Attachment: availability_grid_proposal.xlsx (spreadsheet with sample windows and timezone conversions)

From: Marta Silva <marta.silva@medsys.io>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: Jin Park <jin.park@medsys.io>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Tue, 28 Sep 2026 13:40:00 +0100
Subject: Re: Post-call: staggered technical deep-dive — three-shift scheduling options

Thanks Ava — that recap is on point. Quick notes from our side: 

- Confirmed attendees who must be present at the deep-dive: myself (product), Jin Park (infra lead), and our security reviewer may join for the first 30 mins.
- Mandatory: we need the ability to route the session recordings and audit logs to our internal S3/KMS and have a SOC2 discussion on the call.
- Blackout: the week of Oct 11 is problematic for our R&D sprint-ending testing.

Regarding cadence, leaning toward option 2 (fixed-week blocks) because it balances continuity for engineers and reduces repeated onboarding for the same group. Proposed initial block:
- Week 1 (EU block): Wed 14:00 CET, Oct 6
- Week 2 (US block): Tue 10:00 ET, Oct 12 (but note blackout, so prefer Oct 13)
- Week 3 (APAC block): Thu 09:00 SGT, Oct 21

I added Jin on this thread — he will confirm availability for the APAC slot. If you can publish a calendar invite that rotates ownership (so we have the right joins), that would be ideal.

On quoting Ava's attachment: the availability grid looks good — if you can export the proposed rotation as a PDF and a one-sheet agenda that lists the security items up front, we can circulate internally.

Thanks,
Marta
Product Lead, Platform Integrations
Medsys Health
marta.silva@medsys.io

title: Interchange: Deep dive calendar choreography
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.ai>
To: Marta Lopez <marta@medisync.com>, Oliver Chen <oliver.chen@medisync.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>, Jasmine Liu <jasmine@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 25 Sep 2026 14:10:00 -0700
Subject: Interchange: Deep dive calendar choreography

Hi Marta / Oliver —

Great conversation today — thanks for taking the time. Quick recap and proposed next step so we keep momentum: 

1) Goal for the deep dive: walk through Redwood Dedicated performance profile for Medisync’s multi-region inference workload, plus a short ops Q&A (30–45 min).
2) Attendees we expect: your infra lead (APAC), product (EU), and the Medisync security contact; we’ll bring Ben (solutions), Jasmine (platform), and me.
3) Prep: I attached a short prep doc with suggested sample prompts and expected throughput numbers (deepdive-prep.pdf).

Calendar ask: to avoid repeated rescheduling across time zones, would you be open to a 3-week rotating window where each meeting prioritizes one region (week 1: US-friendly time, week 2: EU-friendly, week 3: APAC-friendly), then repeat? The idea is a recurring slot shared between teams so local stakeholders get convenient windows on a rotating basis.

If that sounds ok, please tell us which of these options works best for your team (times are in your local zone unless noted):

Option A — US-first cadence (weeks labeled by priority):
 - Week 1 (US): Tue 16:00–17:00 PT / Wed 00:00–01:00 CET
 - Week 2 (EU): Thu 10:00–11:00 CET
 - Week 3 (APAC): Mon 07:00–08:00 SGT

Option B — Compact rotating windows (shorter overlap, tighter for global sync):
 - Week 1 (US): Wed 15:00–16:00 PT
 - Week 2 (EU): Tue 09:30–10:15 CET
 - Week 3 (APAC): Thu 06:30–07:15 SGT

Option C — Fixed middle-ground (same moment for all, stretches local times):
 - Every other Thursday 14:00 UTC (works as evening US / late afternoon EU / early morning APAC)

We can also propose a recurring invite and rotate the organizer so the local champion takes ownership each week. If you prefer, send over a handful of concrete availability windows and I’ll map them into a repeating cadence.

Next step: send 1–2 preferred options and we’ll create the calendar series (I can include an .ics and a shared agenda).

Thanks —
Aditya

title: Clarify marketplace participation fees, invoice owner & order form sequencing
preview:
From: Daniel Park <daniel.park@cloudcart.io>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-18T08:12:00-07:00
Subject: Marketplace passthrough / invoice owner question for reseller addendum

Hi Aisha —

Thanks for the call this morning. Were finalizing our marketplace onboarding and the legal team has a couple of quick asks before we move to signature on the reseller addendum + order form:

- Can Redwood accept a fees pass-through model where CloudCart remits marketplace fees to the underlying cloud provider and invoices Redwood net of that fee?
- If not, who is the invoice owner for consumption billed via the marketplace (CloudCart or Redwood)?
- Wed like a clear sequencing note in the order form: purchase -> activation -> marketplace settlement (30 days). Can you confirm Redwoods ops can support that waterfall?

Attached: OrderForm_Draft_CloudCart.pdf (sample).

Wed appreciate a short bullet list we can share with our procurement and tax teams today. Goal: close the purchase order and hit production next week.

Thanks,
Daniel
Director, Strategic Partnerships
CloudCart

---


From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@cloudcart.io>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-19T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Marketplace passthrough / invoice owner question for reseller addendum

Hi Daniel —

Thanks for outlining this. Quick answers up front and a proposed next steps list so we can get you a redline and a clear ops mapping by Thursday:

Short answers:
- Fees pass-through: acceptable in principle, but legal needs the specific pass-through clause in the addendum (tax, audit, and indemnity carveouts) — Marissa will send redlines.
- Invoice owner: we can map either way (CloudCart invoices customers; Redwood invoices CloudCart) but finance will need the waterfall and settlement timing spelled out.
- Sequencing: Redwoods Dedicated/Marketplace onboarding can support purchase -> activation -> settlement (30 days), but we need the Order Form to show which party owns subscription entitlement and who receives usage credits.

Proposed next steps: 
1) Marissa to provide legal redline focused on pass-through language and audit scope (timeline: 1 business day).
2) Ben to produce an ops sequencing sheet showing order flow and settlement handoffs (Ben: can you confirm by EOD?).
3) Laura to confirm finance implications (invoice owner responsibilities, VAT handling).
4) Well return a consolidated addendum + annotated Order Form for your procurement and tax teams.

If you can confirm procurement contact (copy them if helpful), well flag the DocuSign request after legal sign-off.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Account Executive, Strategic GTM
Redwood Inference

> Thanks for the call this morning. Were finalizing our marketplace onboarding and the legal team has a couple of quick asks before we move to signature on the reseller addendum + order form:...

From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@cloudcart.io>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-20T10:15:00-07:00
Subject: Reseller addendum redline (pass-through + audit carveouts)

Daniel / Aisha —

Attached is a focused redline (Reseller_Addendum_Redline_v3.docx). Key points called out in the redline:

- Pass-through clause: we accept a pass-through with the condition that CloudCart confirms the exact fee elements being passed through (line-item detail) and agrees to maintain supporting records for 3 years.
- Invoice owner and entitlement: the Order Form must specify who holds the subscription entitlement. If CloudCart remains the invoice owner, we require a limited audit right for settlement reconciliation and a clause shifting indemnity for third-party billing errors back to CloudCart.
- Tax and VAT: add a VAT handling paragraph that ensures the invoice shows VAT as a separate line and clarifies reverse-charge responsibilities per jurisdiction.
- Audit scope: restrict audits to settlement reconciliation, with 30 days notice and two audits per year maximum unless fraud suspected.

Please review the redline. Marissa included tracked comments where Ive requested sample settlement examples from Ben and Laura. If CloudCart can provide a sample invoice and a 30-day settlement flow, we can finalize language quickly.

Att: Reseller_Addendum_Redline_v3.docx (core redlines)

Thanks,
Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Commercial
Redwood Inference

> > Quick answers up front and a proposed next steps list so we can get you a redline and a clear ops mapping by Thursday:...

---


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Daniel Park <daniel.park@cloudcart.io>, Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwood.ai>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>, Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-21T11:22:00-07:00
Subject: Order form sequencing + sample settlement waterfall

Folks —

I put together a short sequencing workbook (attached) that shows how orders would flow if CloudCart is the marketplace invoice owner vs. Redwood being invoice owner. Highlights: 

- If CloudCart invoices end customers: CloudCart collects marketplace fees, remits net to Redwood on a 30-day settlement window. Redwood invoices CloudCart for net usage.
- If Redwood invoices customers (marketplace passes billing through to Redwood): Redwood will need a contractual representation from CloudCart that customer entitlements were properly recorded and CloudCart will provide monthly settlement attachments.
- Operational constraints: our billing system only supports one invoice owner per HubSpot subscription record. For blended flows we need a mapping table in the Order Form (see tab: "entitlement-mapping").

Action items: 
- CloudCart: please attach an example invoice showing the marketplace fee line and indicate what fields you need present in Redwoods invoice for settlement reconciliation.
- Aisha: once we have that example Ill finalize the sequencing sheet and call out the HubSpot config change needed.

Att: OrderForm_Sequence.xlsx (contains 2 sample flows).

Thanks,
Ben Carter
Sales Operations Manager
Redwood Inference

Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-09-21T14:40:00-07:00
Subject: Finance view: invoice ownership and VAT handling

Daniel / team —

Short finance notes: 

- Invoice owner: finance is comfortable with either mapping as long as the Order Form includes: invoice owner field, currency per invoice, settlement lead time (30 days), and a named contact for reconciliations.
- VAT/withholding: we need CloudCart to confirm the tax jurisdiction for each customer instance. If CloudCart acts as reseller and collects VAT, Redwood will issue net invoices to CloudCart. If Redwood is the invoice owner, Redwood will apply VAT per customer jurisdiction and CloudCart must provide tax exemption certificates where applicable.
- Settlement attachments: please include the following per settlement: invoice id, usage dates, token counts, marketplace fee breakdown, and customer identifier. These are required for our AR automation.

HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/deals/12345/DEAL-2026-0841 (for billing mapping).

If CloudCart can share a sample invoice by EOD tomorrow, well confirm final language for the Order Form and finance will provision the settlement feed.

Laura Bennett

Redwood Inference

title: FastRetail - Trial success plan & week 1 commitments
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex@redwood.ai>
To: Sabrina Cole <sabrina@fastretail.com>, Tom Nguyen <tnguyen@fastretail.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.ai>
Date: Fri, 11 Sep 2026 16:12:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap + proposed week 1 commitments (FastRetail trial)

Hi Sabrina / Tom — thanks again for a focused conversation today. Quick recap and a proposed set of commitments to get the 2‑week trial running with clear success signals. Please flag anything off.

Summary of what we heard
- Goal: Validate Redwood inference for product catalog enrichment (title normalization + attribute extraction) on 500k SKUs by end of trial.
- Success criteria (customer-proposed): precision >= 92% on title normalization; extraction recall >= 88% on core attributes; end-to-end latency per SKU (batch) <= 120ms median.
- Data: you’ll provide a 50k sample and a labeled subset (~5k) for evaluation.

Proposed week 1 commitments (kickoff -> prime the trial):
1) Data handoff & access (owner: FastRetail / Tom) — share S3 link and example labeling schema by Mon 9/14 EOD.
2) Infra checklist (owner: Ben) — confirm VPC peering + firewall rules; Ben will share the network_requirements_summary by Mon.
3) Success criteria sheet (owner: Alex) — we attached a template; please confirm metrics and thresholds by Tue 9/15.
4) Runbook + test harness (owner: Redwood / Marissa) — initial runbook and scripts attached (FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf).
5) Weekly cadence (owner: Alex) — 30m sync Thursdays 10:00am PT; weekly status doc updated via Drive.

Risks & mitigations
- Data sensitivity: if any PII exists we’ll enable token filtering and use private deployment wiring for the trial dataset.
- Edge schema mappings: Ben will allocate time to resolve mapping gaps during the first 48 hours after ingest.

Next steps (actionable)
- Tom: S3 share + IAM role for redwood-poc@redwood.ai by Mon 9/14.
- Sabrina: confirm acceptance thresholds on the attached spreadsheet by Tue 9/15.
- Ben: post network checklist by Mon 9/14.
- Marissa: schedule initial test run for Wed 9/16.

Attachments: FastRetail_success_criteria_sheet.xlsx (xls), FR_POC_runbook_v1.pdf (pdf), network_requirements_summary.txt (txt)

Thanks — I’ll follow up with a calendar invite for the Thursday sync.

— Alex
Alex Martinez
Account Executive, Redwood Inference

- We’ll share S3 bucket and an IAM role (redwood-poc-access) by EOD 9/14. Tom is looping in our infra (Jared) to help with the role.

- Can Ben share an explicit list of IPs and port ranges for our security team? We have a short procurement window and they need transport details.

Best,

title: Renewal readiness ledger and exec escalation asks for Stratus AI
preview:
From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>

Date: Sat, 12 Sep 2026 09:05:00 -0700
Subject: Renewal readiness ledger and exec escalation asks for Stratus AI

Team — looping you in to lock down prep ahead of the 9/21 customer QBR and the renewal discussion. Stratus is a mid‑enterprise account with a potential renewal on 2026‑10‑15 (deal RDW‑3471). Per leadership ask, we need a short ledger that: current health, delta to target ARR, outstanding blockers, and any recommended exec flags.

Quick summary (from initial ops check):
- Current committed ARR: $480k annualized.
- Usage vs. commitment: 78% avg across last 30 days; top latent feature is caching misconfig for inference pools.
- Open severity 2 tickets: 3 (two recent regressions from 8/25–9/07).

Please do the following by EOD Mon 9/13:
1) Update the attached renewal_ledger_v1.xlsx with your sections (account health, support risk, recommended ask/discount band).
2) Flag any item that needs exec attention in the ledger with a one‑line rationale.
3) Call out an owner and proposed escalation time (if we should pull in SVP Sales or CTO).

Target deliverable: one‑page exec summary + ledger row per risk, ready for the exec sync on 9/15. I’ll consolidate and send the exec packet.

Notes: we should also confirm that Stratus has the usage report and open to sharing the upcoming cost optimization suggestions; Rebecca, can your team confirm current infra footprint and whether they will provide the 30‑day usage extract?

Thanks — Ben

--
Ben Carter
Senior Account Executive | Redwood Inference
ben.carter@redwood.ai | +1 (415) 555‑0192

- The three open tickets you referenced are under investigation; two are regression results from our rollout on 8/30 and engineering is targeting a patch next week.
- We are looking at an increase in inference batch sizes starting 9/10 which should push utilization up; I’ll include expected uplift numbers in the report.

I’ll attach the usage PDF and a brief note on the upcoming infra changes once I have the export.

From: Elena Torres <elena.torres@healthsync.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
Cc: DevOps Team <platform@healthsync.com>
Date: 2026-09-09T09:14:00-07:00
Subject: MAP request — acceptance evidence we need

Hi Aisha,

Thanks again for the demo last week — the team at HealthSync is excited about Redwood's latency profile and KV-cache options. Before our procurement and security committees move forward we need a concise Mutual Action Plan that: 1) maps each acceptance criterion to a test artifact, 2) lists the owner and run cadence for each test, and 3) includes a rollback/degeneration gate for any regression.

Can you send a MAP that also identifies the expected pass thresholds (quantitative where possible), evidence we can attach to our audit, and who owns each test on Redwood's side? Ideally we can land this in the next week so our legal team can line up contract milestones.

Elena
Elena Torres
VP, Product Operations — HealthSync

--

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>
To: Elena Torres <elena.torres@healthsync.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-09T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP request — acceptance evidence we need

Elena — thanks for the clear ask. I attached an initial MAP workbook (HS-MAP-evidence-trace-v1.xlsx) and a short evaluation-tests.md that enumerates automated and manual checks. Top-level approach:

- Success criteria: concrete pass thresholds per API (p50/p95 latency under target, 99% token integrity on sampling tests, embedding cosine similarity above 0.92 on reference set).
- Exit gates: must pass smoke suite + compliance checklist before canary; if any critical test fails twice in a 24h window we trigger rollback.
- Evidence: test logs (timestamped), curl traces, sample inputs/outputs, model config digest, and a signed test owner acknowledgement.

Quick asks for you: confirm the acceptable latency SLOs (p95 target) and whether HealthSync requires data residency proof (we've noted region us/eu options). Once you confirm, Ben will add infra-level acceptance tests and Marissa will map billing/milestone checkpoints.

Deliverables timeline (draft):
- Today: v1 MAP workbook (attached)
- 2 business days: technical acceptance test list + owners (Ben)
- 4 business days: consolidated MAP + acceptance evidence samples (Marissa/Aisha)

Thanks — looping Ben and Marissa into the thread.

Best,
Aisha
Aisha Rahman
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: HS-MAP-evidence-trace-v1.xlsx, evaluation-tests.md


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwoodinference.com>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha.rahman@redwoodinference.com>, Elena Torres <elena.torres@healthsync.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwoodinference.com>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwoodinference.com>
Date: 2026-09-10T08:36:00-07:00
Subject: Re: MAP request — acceptance evidence we need

Elena / Aisha — I reviewed the workbook and added a technical acceptance tab in the spreadsheet. Highlights:

- Smoke tests (pre-canary): basic generate endpoint sanity, KV cache warmup, auth token validation. Owner: Ben (infra) — cadence: once per canary; artifact: smoke-run-YYYYMMDD.log.
- Performance tests (phase): p50/p95 token latency measured with 1k realistic traces. Pass: p95 < 350ms (us-west). Owner: Ben. Artifact: bench-report-YYYYMMDD.json + traces.
- Quality checks: 500-sample correctness harness (prompt set provided by HealthSync). Pass: >= 92% match on golden set; owner: Monica (ML).
- Regression/rollback policy: any perf regression exceeding 20% vs baseline or a quality drop >5% triggers an immediate rollback and a triage call.

I've stubbed the automated jobs and included sample CLI commands in evaluation-tests.md. Also included a minimal evidence template (evidence/README.md) that lists required headers for logs and a digest of the model binary/config.

Note: we can present the first evidence packet as a read-only Drive folder and share a signed acceptance note after the smoke run.

Ben
Ben Carter
Infrastructure Lead, Redwood Inference

Attachment: evaluation-tests.md

Marissa Cole
Director, Customer Success — Redwood Inference

From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Jordan Hughes <jordan.hughes@apexmed.com>

Date: Tue, 7 Sep 2026 15:22:00 -0700
Subject: Procure/Billing follow-up from today's call

Jordan — thanks again for taking time today. Quick recap and the asks we surfaced on the call so your AP team can evaluate scope: 

- Procurement path: you mentioned centralized PO for larger vendors. Can you confirm whether ApexMed will raise a standard PO per billing period or per purchase order (monthly consolidated PO vs per-deployment PO)?
- Payment method: did you want to pay via PO (wire/ACH after invoice) or corporate card? Any corporate card limits we should know (single-transaction caps)?
- Marketplace interest: are you open to purchasing via your cloud marketplace (GCP Marketplace) if Redwood can be listed? That can simplify procurement for platform teams.
- Invoice requirements: AP asked for the invoice to include: PO number, billing address, tax ID/EIN, VAT breakdown (if applicable), and a single PDF per month with line-item usage. Can you confirm format (PDF is fine, or do you require XML/EDI)?
- Net terms: what is your target net terms (30/45/60)? Any internal approvals needed for 60+ day terms?
- Vendor setup: AP will need Redwood's W9 and vendor form. I attached a sample invoice and a vendor setup checklist for reference.

Attachments: redwood-invoice-sample.pdf (invoice sample, PDF), vendor-setup-checklist.xlsx (checklist, spreadsheet)

Next steps I propose:
1) You confirm PO cadence and preferred payment method.
2) I drop the W9 and our standard vendor contact info once you confirm you need it.
3) If marketplace is preferred, I loop Karthik for Marketplace onboarding details.

If helpful, we can do a short call with AP and our Billing Ops to walk through the sample invoice and vendor form. When works for you?

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Senior Sales Manager | Redwood Inference
ava.chen@redwood.com | (415) 555-0192

--
Redwood Inference
