title: Data locality playbook and temporary replication approval
preview:
From: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@datahealth.co>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Legal Team <legal@datahealth.co>
Date: 2026-08-01T09:12:00-07:00
Subject: Temporary replication window for analytics workload

Hi Aisha / Grace,

Quick one — our analytics team needs a short-lived replica of EU customer traffic in a US analytics cluster for a one-off model training run next week. DataHealth must keep any cross-border copies for the shortest practical period; performance for the training job requires local compute in the US for a 48–72 hour window.

Can Redwood provide a documented runbook for:
- the exact retention window for temporary replicas,
- encryption at rest and key controls,
- subprocessors that would process the replicated data, and
- an audit trail we can hand to our privacy team?

We're aiming for the model training on Aug 6–7; please let me know what evidence you can provide and any formal signoffs you need.

Thanks,
Sarah
Sarah Liu
Head of Data Platforms | DataHealth

Attachment: training-job-schedule.xlsx (attachment stub)

From: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>
To: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@datahealth.co>
Cc: Grace Oconnor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>, Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-01T11:02:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary replication window for analytics workload

Sarah — thanks for the heads up. We can support a short-lived replication for analytics with a few commitments, but we need to get the operational and legal knobs lined up. High-level proposal from our side: 

- Temporary replica window: up to 7 days maximum, with default safe option at 72 hours for the training job you described.
- Retention enforcement: automated TTL at the storage layer + forced cache/kv eviction after TTL.
- Encryption: replica will use the same KMS keys as your region; we can restrict key access to a limited operator role.
- Subprocessors: full subprocessors list + planned transient subprocessor disclosures (we'll include names for any cloud provider services used).
- Audit: we can emit an audit bundle (access logs, replica creation + deletion events) suitable for your privacy team.

I'll loop in Ben for the ops details and Marissa for contract-side language we should put into an addendum. I attached a light runbook draft to kick off this conversation.

Best,
Aisha Rahman
Enterprise Sales Manager | Redwood Inference

Attachment: data-locality-runbook-v1.md (draft)

From: Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai>
To: Aisha Rahman <aisha_rahman@redwood.ai>, Grace Oconnor <grace_oconnor@redwood.ai>
Cc: Sarah Liu <sarah.liu@datahealth.co>, Marissa Cole <marissa_cole@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-08-02T08:50:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Temporary replication window for analytics workload

On Mon, Aug 02, 2026 at 08:50 Ben Carter <ben_carter@redwood.ai> wrote:

Aisha / Sarah — quick ops note and constraints: 

- We can spin up a region-bound replica (US-east) that will be created from snapshots of the EU store. Creation time for a normal dataset of your size is ~45–90 minutes.
- We recommend a default TTL of 72 hours for the replica to match your ask; we can extend to 120 hours with approval, but anything >72 hours requires a written exception and added logging retention.
- Replica lifecycle: snapshot -> replica instance -> ingestion -> model run -> verified destroy. The destroy process is synchronous and will emit deletion confirmation to the audit bundle.
- KMS: we can use customer-managed keys (bring-your-own-key) in the US region. If you use Redwood-managed keys, access is limited to a small operator principal; all access is logged.
- Network egress: we'll route via our private peering where available; otherwise standard TLS over public egress with routing metadata captured for the audit.

Operational asks for you: confirm dataset size, the exact window you want (start/end datetimes) and whether you require an escrow of the audit bundle.

Ben
Ben Carter
Head of Infrastructure Ops | Redwood Inference

Attachment: replica-lifecycle-diagram.png (attachment stub)

title: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight
preview:
From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 Aug 2026 10:12:00 -0700
Subject: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Aditya / Marissa,

We're preparing network-side changes for our private deployment and need to hand off the egress/IP allowlist items to our infra team. Before we open a ticket with our network ops, quick clarifying questions:

- What exact IPs or CIDR blocks will Redwood require for outbound traffic from our tenant?
- Do you require a fixed source NAT (SNAT) from our side, or do you accept a NATed pool from our gateway?
- Any specific port ranges or protocols besides HTTPS (443) that need to be opened (e.g., health checks, metrics, agent checks)?

I've attached our current allowlist export template which we can populate once we confirm. Goal is to complete network changes this week so we can start smoke tests next Monday.

Thanks —
Amelia
Network Lead | Brightline Health
amelia.torres@brightline.com
Attachment: egress_ip_allowlist.csv (CSV)



From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 02 Aug 2026 13:03:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Amelia — thanks for the clear list. High level answers up front, then an attached preflight checklist we use for every private deployment.

- Redwood outbound endpoints: we use a small, stable set of IPs for control-plane and telemetry. For Brightline we will provide the exact /32 list once you confirm the environment name; placeholder below.
- SNAT: we prefer a stable source IP per tenant (SNAT) so our servers can maintain simple allowlist rules. If your gateway provides a Pooled NAT, we can still work with it but will need the exact ephemeral pool and a short-lived testing cadence.
- Ports/protocols: primary requirement is HTTPS (443). Additionally:
  - TCP 443 outgoing to our control endpoints (required)
  - TCP 443 incoming is not required unless you run a webhook; for agent callbacks we use outbound calls from Redwood.
  - ICMP for simple ping health checks is optional; we rely on HTTPS endpoints for readiness.

Please see the attached "env-validation-checklist.pdf" — it includes the fields we need filled (vpc id, nat gateway allocation, subnet CIDRs, peering info, DNS entries, and ACL rules). I pasted the quick allowlist example below.

Sample egress list (placeholder):
- 52.23.101.19/32  (control.redwood-inference.com)
- 34.89.210.45/32  (metrics.redwood-inference.com)
- 18.197.64.12/32  (artifact-downloads.redwood-inference.com)

Next steps:
1) If you can attach the completed CSV with your VPC NAT IP(s), we'll validate and return the final /32 list.
2) Ben (cc) will outline test steps for connectivity and a simple curl-based smoke test.

Thanks —
Aditya Rao
Manager, Solutions Engineering | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwood.com
Attachment: env-validation-checklist.pdf (PDF)



From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 08:14:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Aditya — attaching our filled CSV (egress_ip_allowlist.csv). We have two NAT IPs from our primary region and one secondary for failover. We can provide a static SNAT if that is preferred; ops prefers configuring a single elastic IP per NAT gateway.

Questions from our ops lead:
- Do you validate TLS client certs or IP-based allowlisting is sufficient?
- Will Redwood initiate connections to any ports other than 443 for the agent/heartbeat?

Proposed go-live window: Thu Aug 6th (after your validation). Let me know if that works.

Cheers,
Amelia
Attachment: egress_ip_allowlist.csv (CSV)



From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 09:22:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Hi Amelia — Ben on infra here. Quick technical clarifications and recommended rules you can hand to your firewall team: 

1) TLS/client auth: for this deployment we do not require mTLS at the network layer. IP allowlisting + TLS server validation is sufficient. Application-layer tokens are used for per-tenant auth. If you prefer mTLS we can support it but that requires cert distribution and is out of scope for this initial cutover.

2) Ports: only outbound TCP 443 is needed. For the smoke tests we also reference 443 for health endpoints. No inbound ports required from Redwood IPs.

3) Suggested firewall ACL entries (egress allowed):
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 52.23.101.19/32 port 443  (control API)
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 34.89.210.45/32 port 443  (metrics/telemetry)
  - ALLOW tcp from <tenant-subnet> to 18.197.64.12/32 port 443  (artifacts)

4) NAT guidance: single elastic IP per NAT gateway is ideal. If you have a HA pair with active/standby, please provide both IPs and mark the active one – we will accept both but prefer a stable primary.

5) Smoke test steps (run from a host in the tenant subnet):
  - curl -v https://control.redwood-inference.com/healthz (should return 200 and JSON with "status":"ok")
  - curl -v https://metrics.redwood-inference.com/ping (200 expected)
  - traceroute -T -p 443 control.redwood-inference.com (confirm routing path)

If you'd like, we can run the smoke tests with you on a quick call. Otherwise, once you confirm ACLs and we've validated your CSV, we'll issue the final /32 list and sign off.

Best,
Ben Carter
Senior Infra Engineer | Redwood Inference
ben@redwood.com



From: Amelia Torres <amelia.torres@brightline.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Aditya Rao <aditya@redwood.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 03 Aug 2026 09:46:00 -0700
Subject: Re: Tenant egress & IP allowlist — handoff and preflight

Ben / Aditya — this is great, thank you. Ops confirms they can set a single elastic IP per NAT gateway; I've updated the CSV to mark the active IP. We'll schedule the change window for Thu Aug 6 at 02:00 - 04:00 PDT. Can we do a 30-minute test/validation call at 03:00 PDT? If you confirm, I'll create the change request and share the ticket (HS-987654) and invite the teams.

Also confirming: no inbound rules required and IP allowlist + TLS server auth is acceptable for initial rollout. We'll revisit mTLS in Q4 if needed.

Thanks —
Amelia
Network Lead | Brightline Health
amelia.torres@brightline.com

title: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks
preview:
From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-25T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Hi Ben / Monica (looping Lauren for visibility),

Thanks for joining the discovery with Sara and Tom at HeliosHealth earlier. Quick recap and proposed path so we have a single source of truth for exec/ procurement alignment: 

1) Top pain points they called out
- Inference cost and unpredictability when they move from pilot to clinician-facing volume (peak vs baseline).
- End-to-end latency impacting charting workflows (90th percentile spikes during peak hours).
- Compliance: SOC 2 evidence, data residency requirements for certain PHI-derived features.
- Fear of model drift and quality regressions -- they need rollback/fallback guarantees.

2) Business outcomes they want
- 30% reduction in inference unit cost vs current provider on 3M monthly tokens.
- Sub-200ms median inference latency in their primary region for clinical flows.
- Auditability and log retention for queries used in clinical decisions.

3) Proposed decision path (owner / ETA)
- Redwood to deliver MAP + two cost-band options (conservative / aggressive) — owner: Alex — due: 2026-07-27
- Legal procurement template + redline window estimate — owner: Monica — due: 2026-08-10
- PO target / commercial signoff — owner: Helios procurement (Tom) — target: 2026-08-20 (TBD)
- Pilot kickoff (Dedicated small pool) — owner: CS (TBD) — target: 2026-09-07

4) Open commercial asks from call
- Helios wants 3 cost scenarios (pilot, scaled, and burst) with per-token math and expected savings.
- They asked for a short exec one-pager showing business outcomes and SLA commitments for leadership review.
- Procurement asked for a one-week legal review window; they will provide contract template.

Attachments: helios_map.xlsx (mutual action plan stub), redwood_mup_agreement_v1.pdf (example MUP).

Immediate asks from our side:
- Ben: can you confirm the rough discount bands we can offer at $X commit (ballpark)?
- Monica: can you pre-skim the redline risks we should expect so we can advise Helios on timelines?
- Lauren/CS: identify an onboarding lead we can name in the MAP to shorten governance cycles.

I'll turn this into a short external-facing summary and send to Sara/Tom today with the MAP link.

— Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-25T17:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Alex — thanks for the clear notes. Quick direction:

- Good plan. Produce the MAP and two cost bands (nominal and aggressive). We should model at 3M tokens/mo and 10M tokens/mo to show scale delta.
- I'll propose a high-level commercial band: ~20% discount at modest commit, ~35% at a two-quarter committed baseline (we'll firm numbers after we pull unit economics).
- Monica — please flag any absolute stops (e.g., data residency, mandatory HSM, or unacceptable indemnity language). If legal wants to push a longer review, call it out so we can negotiate timeline.
- Alex: CC CS (Lauren) on the external note and ask for a named onboarding POC.

Thanks — cc-ing Lauren.

Subject: Re: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Hi Sara and Tom — following up with the concise exec/ procurement summary we discussed. Thank you again for the time.

TL;DR: Redwood can support the pilot and the reliability controls you asked for. Below is a proposed decision path and asks to help your leadership & procurement move quickly.

What we heard (short):
• Cost unpredictability when volume spikes; need predictable unit economics.
• Latency constraints for clinician workflows (90th percentile spikes).
• Compliance: SOC 2 artifacts and US-only residency for specific datasets.

Proposed path and dates (please confirm):
• Redwood delivers MAP + two cost bands (conservative / aggressive) — by 2026-07-29 (link below).
• Helios to confirm PO target date — suggested target: 2026-08-20.
• Legal review window — Helios to advise, we estimate 1–2 weeks for standard redlines.
• Pilot (Dedicated) kickoff — target 2026-09-07.

Immediate asks for your team: 
1) Please confirm whether the PO target above (Aug 20) is realistic or if we should move to late Aug.
2) Share any mandatory contract clauses up front (data residency/HSM/retention) so Monica can pre-scan.
3) Confirm meeting with your procurement + VP IT (suggest Wed 2026-08-04, 30 min) so we can present the MAP and one-pager to execs.

Links/attachments:
- MAP (editable): https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1a2b3c-Helios-MAP
- Example MUP: redwood_mup_agreement_v1.pdf (attached)
- HubSpot deal: https://app.hubspot.com/contacts/1234567/deal/HLX-2026-274

Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai



From: Sara Klein <sara.k@helioshealth.com>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Tom Hollis <tom.h@helioshealth.com>, Marcus Lee <marcus.lee@helioshealth.com>
Date: 2026-07-28T09:03:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Alex — thanks for the crisp summary. I confirmed with Tom and Marcus (VP IT) on the timeline and high-level asks. A few updates from our side: 

1) Procurement timeline adjustment: we need a slightly longer PO window. Targeting PO issuance by 2026-08-30 now (legal wants two full weeks for redlines).
2) Risks that will be triaged during legal/IT review: 
   - SOC 2 Type II evidence (must be available before pilot kickoff).
   - Data residency: the subset of PHI-derived telemetry must remain in US-East; please confirm how Redwood isolates region-specific data.
   - Encryption: at-rest and in-transit encryption details including key management (we prefer KMS/HSM options).
3) Requests: 
   - Can you provide a short 1–2 slide executive summary that maps business outcomes to the SLA/latency targets? We'll use this in the leadership packet.

From: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>, Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Cc: Lauren Bishop <lauren.bishop@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-25T15:12:00-07:00
Subject: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Hi Ben / Monica (looping Lauren for visibility),

Thanks for joining the discovery with Sara and Tom at HeliosHealth earlier. Quick recap and proposed path so we have a single source of truth for exec/ procurement alignment: 

1) Top pain points they called out
- Inference cost and unpredictability when they move from pilot to clinician-facing volume (peak vs baseline).
- End-to-end latency impacting charting workflows (90th percentile spikes during peak hours).
- Compliance: SOC 2 evidence, data residency requirements for certain PHI-derived features.
- Fear of model drift and quality regressions -- they need rollback/fallback guarantees.

2) Business outcomes they want
- 30% reduction in inference unit cost vs current provider on 3M monthly tokens.
- Sub-200ms median inference latency in their primary region for clinical flows.
- Auditability and log retention for queries used in clinical decisions.

3) Proposed decision path (owner / ETA)
- Redwood to deliver MAP + two cost-band options (conservative / aggressive) — owner: Alex — due: 2026-07-27
- Legal procurement template + redline window estimate — owner: Monica — due: 2026-08-10
- PO target / commercial signoff — owner: Helios procurement (Tom) — target: 2026-08-20 (TBD)
- Pilot kickoff (Dedicated small pool) — owner: CS (TBD) — target: 2026-09-07

4) Open commercial asks from call
- Helios wants 3 cost scenarios (pilot, scaled, and burst) with per-token math and expected savings.
- They asked for a short exec one-pager showing business outcomes and SLA commitments for leadership review.
- Procurement asked for a one-week legal review window; they will provide contract template.

Attachments: helios_map.xlsx (mutual action plan stub), redwood_mup_agreement_v1.pdf (example MUP).

Immediate asks from our side:
- Ben: can you confirm the rough discount bands we can offer at $X commit (ballpark)?
- Monica: can you pre-skim the redline risks we should expect so we can advise Helios on timelines?
- Lauren/CS: identify an onboarding lead we can name in the MAP to shorten governance cycles.

I'll turn this into a short external-facing summary and send to Sara/Tom today with the MAP link.

— Alex Martinez
Account Executive | Redwood Inference
alex.martinez@redwood.ai | +1 415-555-0192



From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.ai>
To: Alex Martinez <alex.martinez@redwood.ai>
Cc: Monica Patel <monica.patel@redwood.ai>
Date: 2026-07-25T17:05:00-07:00
Subject: Re: Exec liaison: decision path, procurement timeline, and open asks

Alex — thanks for the clear notes. Quick direction:

- Good plan. Produce the MAP and two cost bands (nominal and aggressive). We should model at 3M tokens/mo and 10M tokens/mo to show scale delta.
- I'll propose a high-level commercial band: ~20% discount at modest commit, ~35% at a two-quarter committed baseline (we'll firm numbers after we pull unit economics).
- Monica — please flag any absolute stops (e.g., data residency, mandatory HSM, or unacceptable indemnity language). If legal wants to push a longer review, call it out so we can negotiate timeline.
- Alex: CC CS (Lauren) on the external note and ask for a named onboarding POC.

Thanks — cc-ing Lauren.

title: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)
preview:
From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Emma Rodriguez <emma@medtechsolutions.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 09:12:00 -0000
Subject: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)

Hi Emma,

Thanks again for a productive call this morning — appreciate you and Ron walking us through the production constraints. Quick recap and the asks we agreed to: 

- Current priorities: process PHI, steady-state ~10k requests/day, burst up to ~250 QPS.
- Key requirements you emphasized: tenant isolation (no cross-tenant KV leakage), audit logs for inference requests, and a data residency option.
- Target SLOs you mentioned: 99.9% availability and P50 generation latency <= 150ms for the primary inference path.

Open questions from our side that will shape the recommended deployment:
1) Do you consider processing PHI a hard requirement for Private deployment, or would Dedicated (with a customer-specific reserved pool + DPA) be acceptable if we can guarantee isolation?
2) Do you have an internal encryption/KMS requirement (customer-managed key) or would Redwood-managed keys be acceptable for the POC?
3) Any compliance paperwork we should start early (POC typically needs a redacted data mapping and either SOC2 or an attestation).

Proposed next steps:
- We can stand up a two-week Dedicated evaluation (isolated capacity with guaranteed GPU pools) using a sanitized dataset to validate noisy-neighbor behavior and latency distribution.
- If you need full PHI controls, we can scope a Private VPC deployment for a longer evaluation — that takes a bit more lead time.
- I'll loop in Laura (solutions) and Ben (dedicated ops) to draft the POC plan and provide SOC2 summary.

Are you available for a short 30-minute POC kickoff on Wed 21 Jul (US/Eastern) or Thu 22 Jul?

Thanks,
Rishi

--
Rishi Malhotra
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference
rishi@redwood.com

Attachment: redwood_poc_plan.pdf (application/pdf)


From: Emma Rodriguez <emma@medtechsolutions.com>
To: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
Cc: Ron Smith <ron.smith@medtechsolutions.com>
Date: Sun, 19 Jul 2026 14:03:21 -0000
Subject: Re: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)

Thanks, Rishi — great summary. A few clarifications from our side: 

- PHI is a hard requirement for production. For the POC we can use de-identified data but we need to validate controls before we move to prod.
- We strongly prefer customer-managed KMS keys for production; for POC Redwood-managed is acceptable as long as we have a migration path.
- Our baseline SLO ask is 99.95% monthly availability and p50 <= 150ms; p95 target is <= 400ms. If Dedicated gives a consistent p95 we can be comfortable.

Noisy neighbor is our biggest operational fear — we want clear isolation boundaries and a traceable impact analysis if a neighbor causes tail latency. Can the POC include a simple \"traffic spike\" test where you drive synthetic load on a co-located tenant and we watch metrics?

We'll need a copy of your SOC2 summary and a short DPA template to share with legal. Ron and I are available Wed 21 Jul 10:00-11:30 ET for the kickoff.

Emma Rodriguez
Head of Engineering
MedTech Solutions
emma@medtechsolutions.com
+1 (415) 555-0123

From: Rishi Malhotra <rishi@redwood.com>
To: Emma Rodriguez <emma@medtechsolutions.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 20 Jul 2026 10:22:45 -0000
Subject: Re: Tenancy & isolation requirements — MedTech Solutions (follow-up + POC plan)

Emma / Ron — great, thanks for the availability. Looping in Laura (solutions lead) and Ben (dedicated ops) to scope the two-week Dedicated evaluation and the traffic-spike test. See proposed plan below.

POC proposal (two-week Dedicated evaluation) — goals and high level: 
- Goal: Validate isolation, measure p50/p95 latency under representative workload, and exercise audit/logging and KMS flows.
- Environment: Dedicated reserved GPU pool (isolated nodes, non-shared tenancy) with a single customer namespace; Redwood will enable request-level audit logs and provide a metrics dashboard.
- Tests to run:
  1) Baseline latency profile with sanitized dataset (3 days of steady traffic).
  2) Noisy neighbor spike: we will introduce synthetic cross-tenant load on a co-located tenant to observe tail-latency impact.
  3) Failover: simulated node restart to validate graceful degradation and cache-warm behaviors.
  4) KMS flow: end-to-end check using Redwood-managed keys for POC; document steps for customer-managed KMS for production planning.
- Deliverables: test runbook, p50/p95 graphs, audit log samples, SOC2 summary, DPA template, recommended next-step path for Private VPC if required.

Logistics & timing:
- Kickoff: Wed 21 Jul 10:00 ET (you confirmed) — 30m.
- Prep: Redwood will share POC runbook and access instructions 24 hours prior.
- Start: Thu 22 Jul — run baseline tests and noisy-neighbor spike during second week.

Ben / Laura, can you confirm capacity: we need a small Dedicated pool (2 GPUs, NVIDIA A10/A30-class equivalent) reserved for two weeks starting Thu 22 Jul. Also attaching a draft POC plan and SOC2 summary for Emma to review.

Emma — attached are: redwood_poc_plan.pdf (draft runbook) and soc2-summary.pdf (SOC2 scope and attestation summary). I'll also share a short DPA template if legal wants it.

Thanks,
Rishi

--
Rishi Malhotra
Enterprise Sales
Redwood Inference

title: Request: Quarterly invoicing + Net-75 term for Quadrant Health (HQ-2026-042)
preview:
From: Sophia Bennett <sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 27 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -07:00
Subject: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Hi Ava,

Thanks again for the conversation this morning. As discussed, Quadrant Health's fiscal calendar is moving to a quarterly remittance model for large vendor contracts. We are requesting the following non-standard billing terms for the engagement under contract HQ-2026-042:

- Move from monthly invoices to quarterly invoices (one invoice per quarter)
- Change payment terms from Net-30 to Net-75
- Option to split quarterly invoices into up to 2 milestones if required by procurement

We recognize this is outside typical Redwood terms. The driver is their treasury consolidation project; they can only process quarterly vendor schedules for any vendor above $50k ARR.

Marco Ruiz (copied) is Quadrant's procurement lead and can provide any additional paperwork. If you need a formal procurement request I can loop in our legal / procurement team.

Attached: Quadrant_procurement_memo.pdf (summary of policy)

Best,

Sophia Bennett
Director, Vendor Relations
Quadrant Health
[sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com]

---


From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 27 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Ben / Marissa — forwarding Quadrant's request. 

Summary: they want quarterly invoicing + Net-75 (reason: treasury consolidation). Deal size ~ $420k ARR, first year committed. They asked if we can split a quarterly invoice into 2 milestone invoices (they prefer one, but procurement might insist).

Suggested trade-offs we could consider:
- Require 25% prepayment at kickoff and then quarterly invoices + Net-75 on the balance, or
- Offer Net-75 in exchange for a 2% cash discount if they pay within 30 days of invoice, or
- Stick to Net-60 instead of Net-75 and allow quarterly invoices.

Need: quick decision — Quadrant wants an answer by Monday EOD their time (Tue morning PT). Can you advise on finance tolerance and whether this needs exec sign-off? I'm happy to own commercial language and capture amendment in DocuSign.

Thanks,
Ava

[Attachment: Quadrant_procurement_memo.pdf] 

On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 9:12 AM Sophia Bennett <sophia.bennett@quadranthealth.com> wrote:
> (original message pasted)


From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
Cc: Vivek Kulkarni <vivek.kulkarni@redwood.com>
Date: Sun, 28 Jun 2026 02:15:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Fwd: Request: Quarterly invoicing & Net-75 term (HQ-2026-042)

Ava / Marissa — thanks for flagging. I support accommodating Quadrant given strategic nature (Healthcare vertical pilot) but only with mitigations. Proposal for approval: 

1) Require 25% upfront (non-refundable) at SOW signature; remainder invoiced quarterly.
2) Approve Net-75 on the quarterly invoices only if Quadrant accepts a 2% early-payment discount (pay within 30 days) or if they accept electronic ACH only.
3) Limit the concession to a 12-month term; revert to standard billing cadence after renewal unless explicitly re-approved.

I've attached a redline to the standard SOW marking the prepayment and discount language. If Finance gives the cashflow OK, I can present to Vivek for exec sign-off.

-Ben

[Attachment: qhealth_contract_redline.pdf]

On Sat, Jun 27, 2026 at 11:03 AM Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com> wrote:
> (forwarded message)


From: Marissa Cole <marissa.cole@redwood.com>
To: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>, Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>

Team — quick finance read.

Net-75 + quarterly invoicing increases DSO risk materially. With a $420k ARR figure, this could push receivables out by >60 days vs. Net-30. That said, the 25% upfront significantly mitigates exposure. If we do this, I recommend: 

- 25% prepayment at SOW signature (recognize per ASC guidance on delivery terms)
- Quarterly invoices for the balance with Net-60 as a preference (Net-75 only if we include a 2% early-payment discount or other credit protection)
- Add a clause allowing us to invoice immediately for any professional services or onboarding expenses to improve cashflow.

I've attached a proposed invoice schedule (shows timing and expected cashflow) and created BILL-2117 to track accounting changes and revenue recognition notes. Legal should sign off on the redline Ben attached.

If you want to escalate, Vivek — Ben is asking for exec approval for the Net-75 concession tied to the prepayment and discount.

-Marissa

title: Insurance-backed penalty proposal & stepdown thresholds for SLA breaches
preview:
From: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
Cc: Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Wed, 10 Jun 2026 09:15:00 -0700
Subject: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Ava —

Thanks again for the demo last week. Heliox is framing our procurement ask around not just credits but an enforceable upside for us if SLA misses are repeated. Rather than escrow or large immediate termination rights, procurement proposed an insurance-backed construct that pays capped penalties after sustained breaches. Attached is a simple model for the mechanics (heliox-request-model.xlsx). Key asks: 

- Insurance-backed fund: insurer issues a performance bond that pays penalties up to 6% of monthly committed fees per month in the worst tier.
- Stepdown thresholds: penalties escalate after 3 consecutive weekly SLO misses (tier 1), and again after 6 consecutive misses (tier 2) with higher payout.
- Termination: right to terminate only after 3 months of tier-2 level breaches, not on a single incident.

We prefer this instead of a straight termination clause or credit-only approach. Can Redwood confirm feasibility and any regulatory or accounting concerns? Also looping in procurement for visibility.

Tom
Tom Baxter
Director, Procurement
Heliox Therapeutics

Attachments: heliox-request-model.xlsx (spreadsheet)

From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Thu, 11 Jun 2026 11:02:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds

Tom —

Appreciate the clear framing and the model — that helps a lot. Short answer: the concept is something we can work with, but there are a few practical considerations on our side (insurance procurement path, capital exposure, and how penalties interact with Dedication vs Hosted plans).

High-level thoughts: 
- Insurance bond is an interesting alternative to escrow; it reduces the cash on our balance sheet but creates administrative overhead. We need to validate whether our insurers will underwrite this with SLA-language tied to external monitoring metrics.
- The stepdown thresholds are reasonable, but we push to define SLO measurement sources (Redwood Console vs Heliox synthetic checks) and a reproducible reconciliation window.
- Termination only after sustained Tier-2 makes sense; we would ask for a cure period and a remediation playbook before termination triggers.

I've attached an initial draft of the clauses we would expect to see (redwood-draft-terms.docx). Ben and Marissa are on copy to weigh in on commercial and legal risk. Can we set a quick 30-min sync next week to walk through the model?

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx (draft terms)

From: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 09:27:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — commercial input

Ava / Marissa —

Quick notes from commercial perspective after reading Tom's model and Ava's draft: 

- Fee-at-risk sizing: Heliox's model assumes up to 6% per month in worst-case payouts. We should counter with a cap tied to committed spend (e.g., max 12% of monthly committed fee per quarter) to protect runway.
- Pricing impact: If an insurer is involved, there will be an effective premium or monitoring fee — we should ask Heliox to accept that premiums cannot be underwritten by Redwood for long-term deals; alternatively a pass-through or shared-cost model.
- Monitoring: Recommend using Redwood Console as source-of-truth for SLA failures but allow Heliox to submit supplemental synthetic evidence with defined window (48 hrs).

I'm fine with the tiered stepdown approach but want to add an automatic review meeting when Tier-1 is hit twice in a rolling 90-day window — gives us time to debug before Tier-2.

Ben
Ben Carter
Commercial Lead, Redwood Inference


From: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 15 Jun 2026 11:45:00 -07:00
Subject: Internal: Heliox insurance-backed ask — legal comments

Team —

Legal thumbs-up in principle with the insurance-backed mechanism; a few required guardrails: 

- Clear metric definitions: \"SLO misses\" must be unambiguous (p99 latency > X ms over a 1-hour window OR availability < 99.5% per week). Avoid vague language that insurers won't accept.
- Indemnity carve-outs: Redwood needs to ensure penalties don't cascade with other indemnities or create double recovery scenarios.
- Audit rights: Redwood will require insurer confirmation language and a process for validating paid claims. We should insist on an explicit insurer contact and SLA for payout timing.
- Termination: recommend keeping a remediation plan and a final 30-day cure period after Tier-2 before termination is available — this aligns with our operational playbook.

I'll draft markups into the redwood-draft-terms.docx and share. Also recommend we propose a shared-cost model for the insurance premium (split 70/30 Heliox/Redwood) or ask Heliox to obtain the bond directly to avoid our balance-sheet exposure.

Marissa Cole
Senior Counsel, Redwood Inference


From: Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com>
To: Tom Baxter <tbaxter@heliox.com>
Cc: Ben Carter <ben@redwood.com>, Marissa Cole <marissa@redwood.com>, Heliox Procurement <procurement@heliox.com>
Date: Thu, 18 Jun 2026 16:40:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Proposal: insurance-backed penalty + tiered stepdown thresholds — Redwood counter

Tom —

Thanks for your patience while we reviewed your proposal. Attached is a concise counter that preserves your intent but addresses Redwood's operational and legal constraints (see summary below and attached risk-stepdown-chart.png). We'd like to move to a short call to nail down the monitoring and insurer mechanics. Proposed summary: 

- Cap: Penalties funded via an insurer bond capped at 10% of a rolling three-month committed fee (reduces volatility).
- Tiers: Tier-1 (3 consecutive weekly SLO misses) — remediation meeting + 2x credits; Tier-2 (6 consecutive) — insurer payout up to capped amount and formal remediation plan.
- Cure & termination: 30-day cure after Tier-2 before termination available; termination only if remediation fails and Tier-2 breaches continue.
- Premiums: Proposed shared-cost model 60% Heliox / 40% Redwood for initial year; revisit on renewal. If Heliox prefers to source insurer directly, Redwood will accept insurer onboarding provided insurer language is pre-approved.

Attachments provided: redwood-draft-terms.docx (updated with our markups), risk-stepdown-chart.png (visual of triggers). Also sharing a DocuSign stub for the commercial letter if you want to fast-track acceptance: https://docusign.com/heli-penalty-proposal/DS-9F3

Can we take 30 minutes next Tue (23rd) or Wed (24th) to finalize? If yes, please pick a slot and we'll get calendar invites out.

Best,
Ava Chen
Senior Account Executive, Redwood Inference

Attachments: redwood-draft-terms.docx, risk-stepdown-chart.png

On Thu, 11 Jun 2026 at 11:02 AM Ava Chen <ava@redwood.com> wrote:
> Tom —
>
> Appreciate the clear framing and the model — that helps a lot. Short answer: the concept is something we can work with, but there are a few practical considerations on our side (insurance procurement path, capital exposure, and how penalties interact with Dedication vs Hosted plans).
>
> [trimmed quoted content for thread clarity]

title: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing
preview:
From: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Cc: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 09:12:00 -0700
Subject: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Jane / HealthLane team,

Thanks again for the conversation this week. Per our roadmap and the Order Form timeline, I wanted to kick off a short working thread to align on three things we need to close before legal redlines: (1) pen-test scope & timing, (2) SOC 2 / attestation delivery cadence, and (3) how evidence will be routed (ticketing + artifact naming).

From Redwood's side:
- We can accept a staging-only penetration test (no production access) and provide ephemeral creds for the window.
- SOC 2: we can provide the latest report plus a scoped list of control mappings; we normally deliver the full report on an annual cadence and can share an interim attestation in some cases.
- Evidence ticketing: we use a single JIRA board for evidence uploads (one ticket per artifact) and can create a templated ticket that maps to your evidence requests.

Attached: security_exhibit_v1.docx (draft exhibit with initial suggested language).

Asks: 
1) Do you have preferred pen-test windows (weeks in June/July)?
2) Is quarterly or annual SOC 2 delivery sufficient for procurement, or do you need something in between during onboarding?
3) Who should be the single point of contact on your side for evidence review?

If it helps we can set a 30-minute call next week to walk through the draft exhibit line-by-line.

Thanks,
Ava Chen
Sr. Customer Success Manager, Redwood Inference
Ava.chen@redwood.com
[Attachment: security_exhibit_v1.docx (docx)]

From: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
To: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>
Cc: HealthLane Security <security@healthlane.com>, Procurement Team <procurement@healthlane.com>
Date: Fri, 5 Jun 2026 11:03:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Hi Ava — thanks for the clear summary.

Quick answers: 
- Pen-test windows: we prefer mid-July (week of July 12) or the first two weeks of August if you need more runway. Staging-only is fine — we'll coordinate scoping details.
- SOC 2 cadence: Procurement prefers annual, but Security will ask for a short attestation during onboarding if there are any gaps; quarterly would be ideal, but not strictly required. For now, annual + onboarding attestation should cover internal approvals.
- POC: I'm the technical POC (Jane Doe) and procurement will be copied for contract steps.

Attaching our SOC 2 assertions spreadsheet to show the mapping we'll want verified.

Can your legal/controls team confirm whether you will accept a 30-day notice period for inspections and whether Redwood requires reciprocal rights? Procurement will need that for the Order Form.

Thanks,
Jane
Director, Security Operations — HealthLane
jane.doe@healthlane.com
[Attachment: HealthLane-soc2-assertions.xlsx (xlsx)]

From: Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Sat, 6 Jun 2026 08:25:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

Jane — appreciate the quick reply and the assertions sheet. A few operational clarifications from engineering/security: 

- Pen-test scope: we'll scope to non-production endpoints only. We'll provide a staging environment with time-limited keys and an agreed test plan. Staging window preference: week of July 12 is workable for us.

- Inspection/notice: operationally, 30 days notice for an inspection or evidence pull is reasonable. For the MSA clause we typically avoid open-ended inspection rights (we ask for bounded windows and purpose-limited access). Attached is a short snippet with suggested pen-test + inspection language you can share with Procurement/legal.

- Evidence routing: recommend we create a single JIRA project (SEC-EVIDENCE-HealthLane) where each artifact (SOC 2 report, control screenshots, configuration exports) is uploaded to a ticket and linked back to your assertions spreadsheet. This keeps evidence immutable and auditable.

Next steps: I'll draft the pen-test clause and the JIRA template today and loop in Neha for legal redlines.

—Ben
Ben Carter
Security Operations — Redwood Inference
[Attachment: proposed_pen_test_clause_snippet.txt (txt)]

From: Laura Bennett <laura.bennett@redwood.com>
To: Jane Doe <jane.doe@healthlane.com>, Ben Carter <ben.carter@redwood.com>
Cc: Ava Chen <ava.chen@redwood.com>, Neha Kapoor <neha.kapoor@redwood.com>
Date: Mon, 8 Jun 2026 09:10:00 -07:00
Subject: Re: Security exhibit kickoff — evidence cadence, pen-test window, and ticketing

title: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive
preview:
From: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>
To: Erin Zhao <erin.zhao@healthsprout.com>
Cc: Marissa Cole <marissa@redwoodinference.com>, Ben Carter <ben@redwoodinference.com>
Date: Wed, 3 Jun 2026 10:15:00 -0700
Subject: Post-call recap: Aligning multi-zone availability for technical deep dive

Hi Erin — thanks again for a productive discovery call this morning. Quick recap and proposed next steps so we can lock a technical deep dive with the right folks (product + infra):

Key takeaways from today's call:
- Primary use case: real-time inference for patient-facing triage (low-latency, audit logs).
- Expected throughput: bursty peaks ~150 rps; trailing average ~25 rps.
- Data constraints: PHI-filtered inputs; prefer VPC/private deployment and KMS integration.
- Success metrics: <150ms p50 inference, predictable monthly spend.

Immediate asks / next steps:
1) Schedule a 60–75 minute technical deep dive with your infra lead (Samir) + one product PM.
2) We'll demo a short, instrumented run on our staging model and walk through routing/rollout options.
3) Marissa will prepare a short checklist (compliance / data flow) and share sample config.

To make scheduling straightforward across your US/EU/APAC stakeholders I sketched a lightweight rotating-window approach (keeps the same weekday for continuity, rotates the anchor time so teams in each region get a reasonable local hour once every 3 weeks):

Proposed rotation pattern (example weeks):
- Week A: 10:00 PT / 18:00 CET / 02:00 SGT (good for US morning + EU evening)
- Week B: 14:00 PT / 22:00 CET / 06:00 SGT (shifts later for EU/early APAC)
- Week C: 08:00 PT / 16:00 CET / 00:00 SGT (APAC earlier slot)

If that framing looks ok, could you and Samir indicate which of these anchors works best next week? Alternatively you can pick any open slot on this Calendly and we'll book the deep dive: https://calendly.com/redwood/tech-deep-dive

I'll attach a short list of technical questions and a lightweight agenda so we can keep the session focused. Marissa — can you assemble the short checklist and attach?

Thanks — really looking forward to a deep dive.

Best,
Aditya Rao
Enterprise AE | Redwood Inference
aditya@redwoodinference.com | +1 (415) 555-0142

To: Aditya Rao <aditya@redwoodinference.com>

- Samir (cc'd) is the infra lead and will want the stubbed config + expected resource footprint before the call.
- Our PM (Aisha Kim) will join; I can loop her once we confirm the time.
- Preferred anchors: Week A or Week C (Samir prefers not to do the late-night EU/early APAC slot).

We can do Week A next Tuesday (2026-06-09) at your Week A anchor if that works for you: 10:00 PT / 18:00 CET / 02:00 SGT. Alternatively, if Week A next week is full for you, Week C on 06/10 also works for a shorter 60-minute window.

I'll grab a slot on the Calendly now and share the invite.

— Erin
